# game — comprehensive LLM reference

> An ECS game framework for Moku — Spark-style public API and memory layout, PixiJS v8 rendering
> (with a first-class headless mode), built on @moku-labs/core, composable with @moku-labs/web, with
> a first-class MCP plugin exposing the live runtime to agent clients over stdio, Streamable HTTP,
> and/or an in-page inMemory transport (environment-aware default).

This is the comprehensive reference for `game`. Everything below is grounded in source. The package
is **unpublished** (`name: "game"`, `version: "0.0.0"`). Use `bun` exclusively. Per-plugin READMEs
under `src/plugins/<name>/README.md` are the authoritative API source and remain the canonical
detail; this file consolidates the public surface.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 1. The three-layer Moku model (factory chain)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`game` follows the Moku Core three-layer model. Consumers only ever touch Layer 3.

**Layer 1 — `src/config.ts`** (config + events):

```ts
import { envPlugin, logPlugin } from "@moku-labs/common";
import { createCoreConfig } from "@moku-labs/core";

// The framework defines NO global config fields — all config is per-plugin.
export type Config = {};

// Coarse framework event contract. Hot-path frame work is NOT emitted as kernel events.
export type Events = {
  "assets:loaded": { alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" };
  "scene:loaded": { name: string };
};

export const coreConfig = createCoreConfig<Config, Events, [typeof logPlugin, typeof envPlugin]>(
  "game",
  { config: {}, plugins: [logPlugin, envPlugin] } // → ctx.log + ctx.env on every plugin ctx
);

export const createPlugin = coreConfig.createPlugin;
export const createCore = coreConfig.createCore;
// Re-export the raw Moku Core config factory for advanced / headless assembly.
export { createCoreConfig } from "@moku-labs/core";
```

`logPlugin` and `envPlugin` from `@moku-labs/common` are registered here, so `ctx.log` (structured
logging) and `ctx.env` (validated environment access) are available on every plugin's context.

**Layer 2 — `src/index.ts`** (framework + plugins):

```ts
const framework = createCore(coreConfig, {
  plugins: [
    ecsPlugin, schedulerPlugin, rendererPlugin, inputPlugin,
    loopPlugin, assetsPlugin, contextPlugin, scenePlugin, mcpPlugin
  ],
  pluginConfigs: {
    context: { bindGameContext: true } // framework default; consumers override via createApp
  }
});

export * from "./plugins";            // plugin instances + Ecs/Scheduler/Context/... type namespaces
export const createApp = framework.createApp;
export const createPlugin = framework.createPlugin;

// Advanced / headless escape hatches — re-exported from ./config. `createApp` stays the default.
export { createCore, createCoreConfig } from "./config";
```

**Layer 3 — your app**:

```ts
import { createApp } from "game";

const app = createApp({ pluginConfigs: { /* per-plugin overrides */ } });
await app.start();   // async — boots Pixi Application, registers DOM listeners + systems, binds resources
// app.ecs, app.scheduler, app.renderer, app.input, app.loop, app.assets, app.context, app.scene, app.mcp
await app.stop();    // async — destroys GPU Application, removes listeners, closes MCP server
```

`createApp(...)` returns the App **synchronously**; `app.start()` is **async** and must be awaited
before using lifecycle-owning plugin APIs (renderer/input/loop/context/mcp). `app.ecs` and
`app.scheduler` are usable immediately after `start()` (the ECS world is built eagerly in
`createState`).

**Custom plugins** integrate via `createPlugin` from `game`:

```ts
import { createPlugin } from "game";
import { assetsPlugin } from "game"; // or "game/.../assets" — depend on the instance

const myPlugin = createPlugin("myPlugin", {
  depends: [assetsPlugin],                 // declares the dependency edge
  config: { speed: 1 },                    // per-plugin config (override via pluginConfigs.myPlugin)
  createState: () => ({ ticks: 0 }),       // mutable state object
  api: (ctx) => ({                          // exposed as app.myPlugin
    bump: () => { ctx.state.ticks++; }
  }),
  hooks: (_ctx) => ({                       // subscribe to framework events
    "assets:loaded": ({ alias, kind }) => { /* react */ }
  }),
  lifecycle: {                              // onStart/onStop for resource-owning plugins
    onStart: async (ctx) => {
      const assets = ctx.require(assetsPlugin); // resolve a dependency's API
      // ...
    }
  }
});
```

Resolve a dependency's API at runtime with `ctx.require(<pluginInstance>)`. `depends` declares the
edge (and ordering); `ctx.require` returns the typed API. Resolving lazily inside methods/lifecycle
(not at construction) guarantees the dependency has started.

**Advanced / headless core assembly.** Besides `createApp` / `createPlugin`, `game` re-exports two
Layer-1/2 escape hatches for composing a custom core (tooling, tests, agent-only / headless hosts).
`createApp` remains the default — reach for these only when you need a non-default plugin set or a
bespoke `Config`/`Events` shape.

```ts
import { createCore, createCoreConfig, createPlugin, ecsPlugin, schedulerPlugin, rendererPlugin } from "game";

// (a) createCore — assemble a custom core from a SUBSET of the framework's own plugins. The first
//     argument is a type-only carrier for the bound createPlugin (pass `{ createPlugin }` or `coreConfig`);
//     it is unused at runtime. Returns the framework factory ({ createApp, createPlugin }).
const { createApp } = createCore({ createPlugin }, { plugins: [ecsPlugin, schedulerPlugin, rendererPlugin] });
const app = createApp({ pluginConfigs: { renderer: { headless: true } } });  // headless: no Pixi/GPU
await app.start();   // app.ecs / app.scheduler / app.renderer (Transform defined, render() a no-op)

// (b) createCoreConfig — the raw @moku-labs/core Layer-1 factory, re-exported. Build a bespoke core
//     config with its own Config / Events / core-plugin set, then call the returned createCore.
const myConfig = createCoreConfig("mygame", { config: {}, plugins: [] });
const soloPlugin = myConfig.createPlugin("solo", { api: () => ({ ping: () => "pong" }) });
const mini = myConfig.createCore(myConfig, { plugins: [soloPlugin] }).createApp({});
await mini.start();  // mini.solo.ping() === "pong"
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 2. Plugin dependency graph & initialization order
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Registration order in `src/index.ts` matches the dependency topology:

```
ecs            (no deps — foundation, plugin #1)
 └─ scheduler  (depends: ecs)
     ├─ renderer (depends: ecs, scheduler)
     └─ input    (depends: scheduler)
         ├─ loop    (depends: scheduler, renderer, ecs)
         └─ assets  (depends: renderer)
             ├─ context (depends: ecs, assets)
             └─ scene   (depends: ecs, renderer, assets)
                 └─ mcp (depends: ecs, scheduler, renderer, input, loop, assets, scene — all build deps)

audio          (no deps — foundational, Wave 1; independent of the graph above)
storage        (no deps — foundational, Wave 1; independent of the graph above)
platform       (depends: audio, loop, storage — Wave 2; registered after them, before mcp)
vfx            (depends: ecs, scheduler, renderer — Wave 3; registered after platform, before mcp)
ui             (depends: renderer, scheduler, input — leaf; registered after vfx, before mcp)
tween          (depends: scheduler — leaf; registered after ui, before mcp)
camera         (depends: renderer, scheduler, tween — leaf; registered after tween, before mcp)

# Editor subsystem (Layer-2, additive — plugins #17–#24, registered AFTER mcp; all edges point backwards)
commands          (depends: ecs — the single write-authority; owns the branded EditorId)
reflection        (depends: ecs — field-schema registry)
serialization     (depends: ecs, storage, commands, reflection — scene (de)serializer)
editor-selection  (depends: ecs, renderer, camera, input — viewport picking + selection)
editor-history    (depends: commands — undo/redo)
editor-gizmos     (depends: renderer, camera, editor-selection, commands — translate gizmo)
editor-runtime    (depends: loop, scheduler, serialization, commands, tween, vfx, camera — edit/play FSM)
editor-bridge     (depends: ecs, reflection, commands, editor-selection, editor-gizmos, editor-history, editor-runtime, serialization, mcp — the terminal facade; registered LAST)
```

Build/resolve order: ecs → scheduler → renderer + input → loop + assets → context → scene → audio →
storage → platform → vfx → ui → tween → camera → mcp → commands + reflection → serialization + editor-selection
→ editor-history + editor-gizmos + editor-runtime → editor-bridge (the dependency-free `audio` + `storage`, then `platform` which depends
on `audio` + `loop` + `storage`, then `vfx` on `ecs` + `scheduler` + `renderer`, then `ui` on `renderer` +
`scheduler` + `input`, then `tween` on `scheduler` only, then `camera` on `renderer` + `scheduler` + `tween`,
all registered before `mcp`; then the additive **Layer-2 editor subsystem** #17–#24 after `mcp`, `editor-bridge` last).
The editor subsystem is supported by additive **extension deltas** on already-built plugins: `ecs` gains `changeEpoch()` (per-write counter) + `setActiveStages`/`activeStages` (stage gate); `scheduler` forwards `setActiveStages`/`activeStages`; `assets` gains `entries()`/`manifest()`/`metadata()`; `tween`/`vfx`/`camera` gain `reset()`. All non-breaking; a non-editor game pays nothing (`editor-runtime` never gates until `enterEdit()`).
Each plugin declares `depends: [...]` and resolves the API at runtime with `ctx.require(...)`.
There are no dependency cycles. `loop` depends on `ecs` (to publish the `Time` resource), the
`context` plugin depends on `ecs` + `assets`, and `mcp` depends on its seven build-dependency plugins (its
Cycle 5 tools call into ecs introspection/mutation, the renderer primitive surface, input injection,
loop control, and scene introspection/control). `ecs` still depends on nothing, so adding it as a
`loop`/`context`/`mcp` dependency introduces no cycle.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 3. Framework Config & Events (verbatim public types)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```ts
// src/config.ts
export type Config = {};   // no global config fields; all config is per-plugin

export type Events = {
  "assets:loaded": { alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" };
  "scene:loaded": { name: string };
};
```

The `mcp` and `audio` plugins additionally declare **plugin-level** events (not on the framework `Events`):

```ts
// src/plugins/mcp/types.ts
export type Events = {
  "game:reset": { reason: "mcp" };
};

// src/plugins/audio/types.ts
export type Events = {
  "audio:muteChanged": { muted: boolean };
  "audio:volumeChanged": { channel: "master" | "sfx" | "music"; value: number };
};
```

Event catalog (complete):

| Event | Payload | Declared/Emitted by | When |
|---|---|---|---|
| `assets:loaded` | `{ alias: string; kind: "asset" \| "bundle" }` | framework Events / assets | once per `load()` / `loadBundle()` success; for a bundle `alias` is the bundle name |
| `scene:loaded` | `{ name: string }` | framework Events / scene | after a scene's `setup` completes during `load()` (not on `unload`, not per entity) |
| `game:reset` | `{ reason: "mcp" }` | mcp plugin Events / mcp | after the `game:reset` tool's hard-reset closure runs — every MCP-tracked entity despawned and the current scene unloaded; consumers listen to re-init their own state |
| `audio:muteChanged` | `{ muted: boolean }` | audio plugin Events / audio | after `mute()` / `unmute()` / `setMuted()` changes the mute state (only on an actual change); a `storage`/`platform` plugin persists it |
| `audio:volumeChanged` | `{ channel: "master" \| "sfx" \| "music"; value: number }` | audio plugin Events / audio | after `setVolume()` changes a channel volume (only on change) |
| `platform:ready` | `{ portal: Portal }` | platform plugin Events / platform | once the active portal adapter is initialised and loading is signalled finished |
| `platform:adStart` | `{ type: "interstitial" \| "rewarded" }` | platform plugin Events / platform | when an ad begins, after `loop` is paused + `audio` muted |
| `platform:adEnd` | `{ type: "interstitial" \| "rewarded"; rewarded?: boolean }` | platform plugin Events / platform | when an ad ends, after `loop` + `audio` are restored (`rewarded` set for rewarded ads) |

Design note: per-frame hot-path work (ticks, sync, render, input, structural mutations, resource
ops) is **deliberately not emitted** as kernel events — emitting an event per frame would defeat the
kernel-bypass that keeps the loop fast. ecs, scheduler, renderer, input, loop, context, and vfx emit
no events and register no hooks (vfx's particles/shake/pop/floating-text are all per-frame hot-path
work); `mcp` emits one (`game:reset`) and `audio` two coarse mute/volume
milestones (`play`/`playMusic`/`stopMusic` are hot-path and emit nothing). The `context` plugin hands
systems a typed `emit` (the framework emitter) inside `GameContext`, but owns no event surface of
its own.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 4. ecs — data/runtime core (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`app.ecs` returns the `World` facade **directly** — there is no wrapper layer. The plugin API IS the
World. The world is built eagerly in `createState`, so `app.ecs` is available synchronously after
`start()`. No `onStart`/`onStop` (nothing to open or tear down).

### Public types (src/plugins/ecs/types.ts)

```ts
export type Entity = number & { readonly __entity: unique symbol }; // generational handle
export type StorageStrategy = "archetype" | "sparse";

export type Component<T> = {
  readonly __id: number;
  readonly __value: T;
  (value: T): ComponentInit;     // tokens are callable: Position({x,y}) => ComponentInit
};
export type Tag = Component<Record<never, never>>;        // presence-only marker

// Opaque world-resource token — a typed singleton handle (sibling to Component<T>).
// Keyed by a stable string. __value is a phantom (type-level only) — NEVER set at runtime.
export type Resource<T> = { readonly __key: string; readonly __value?: T };

export type Stage = "input" | "update" | "physics" | "sync" | "render";
export type System = (world: World, dt: number) => void;
export type ComponentInit = { readonly component: Component<never>; readonly value: unknown };

export type Query<Values extends readonly object[]> = {
  updateEach(cb: (values: Values, entity: Entity) => void): void;  // structural ops deferred
  count(): number;
  first(): Entity | undefined;
  [Symbol.iterator](): Iterator<Entity>;
};

export type World = {
  // `opts.name` makes a component discoverable by name through the introspection facet.
  defineComponent<T extends object>(
    create: () => T,
    opts?: { storage?: StorageStrategy; name?: string }
  ): Component<T>;
  defineTag(opts?: { storage?: StorageStrategy; name?: string }): Tag;
  spawn(...parts: ComponentInit[]): Entity;
  despawn(entity: Entity): void;
  isAlive(entity: Entity): boolean;
  add<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>, value?: Partial<T>): void;
  remove<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>): void;
  has<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>): boolean;
  get<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>): T | undefined;
  set<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>, value: Partial<T>): void;
  query<A>(c1: Component<A>): Query<[A]>;            // overloads for arities 1..8
  // ... query(c1..c8) ...
  addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void;
  tick(dt: number): void;

  // ── Introspection (read-only — for tooling such as the mcp plugin) ──
  liveEntities(): readonly Entity[];                                       // fresh snapshot of all live handles (order unspecified)
  entityCount(): number;                                                   // direct count (cheaper than liveEntities().length)
  componentNames(): readonly string[];                                     // names of components defined with opts.name (registration order)
  componentsOf(entity: Entity): ReadonlyArray<{ name: string; value: unknown }>;  // NAMED components on an entity; [] if dead/unknown
  componentByName(name: string): Component<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined;  // name → callable token; undefined for unknown/anonymous; first-wins on dup

  // ── Resources (typed singletons) ──
  defineResource<T>(create?: () => T): Resource<T>;             // mints "res:N" key + optional lazy factory
  setResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>, value: T): void;        // store/replace immediately
  getResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T | undefined;         // lazy-init from factory; undefined if unset+none
  resource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T;                        // same, but THROWS if unset + no factory
  hasResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): boolean;               // true if value set OR factory exists
  removeResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): void;               // clear value; factory re-inits next read
};

export type Config = {
  initialCapacity: number;        // @default 1024
  maxStructuralOpsWarn: number;   // @default 0 (disabled)
};
export type Api = World;          // the plugin API contract IS the World facade
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `initialCapacity` | `number` | `1024` | Pre-allocated entity index slots. Slot table and component columns grow automatically; tuning only avoids early reallocations. |
| `maxStructuralOpsWarn` | `number` | `0` | Warn (via `ctx.log`) past this many structural ops in one flush, to catch runaway spawn storms. `0` disables. Resource ops are NOT counted. |

### Semantics & design

- **Generational entities:** an `Entity` packs a 16-bit index + 16-bit generation as
  `(generation << 16) | index`. `despawn` bumps the slot generation, so stale handles are
  detectably dead via `isAlive` (use-after-free guard). Freed indices go on a `freeList` and are
  recycled.
- **Archetype object-SoA storage:** entities sharing the same sorted component signature live in one
  archetype with parallel columns (one `entities` column + one per component). `despawn` uses
  swap-remove to keep columns dense (O(1)); `add`/`remove` migrate the entity between archetypes.
- **Storage seam:** each component is `"archetype"` (default, cache-coherent) or `"sparse"` (a
  per-component `Map<Entity, value>` — good for high-churn tags/timers). `defineTag` defaults to
  `"sparse"`. Queries return identical results across strategies.
- **Deferred command buffer:** while iterating (inside `updateEach` or any system), `spawn`/
  `despawn`/`add`/`remove` are enqueued, not applied mid-iteration. `spawn` still returns a usable
  `Entity` synchronously (index/generation reserved immediately; insertion deferred). The buffer is
  drained at each stage boundary inside `tick`. Outside iteration, ops apply immediately. This is
  the only structural-mutation path safe during iteration — and the path every `mcp` mutating tool
  uses.
- **Ticking:** `tick(dt)` runs each stage's systems in fixed order `input → update → physics → sync
  → render`, flushing the command buffer between stages. `dt` is the frame delta in seconds.

### Read-only introspection facet

The world exposes five read-only methods so external tooling (notably the `mcp` plugin) can inspect
the live world without touching archetype layout. None mutate; all are safe between or during
frames.

| Method | Signature | Semantics |
|---|---|---|
| `liveEntities` | `liveEntities(): readonly Entity[]` | Fresh snapshot array of every currently-live handle (order unspecified; includes component-less entities). Mutating it does not affect the world. |
| `entityCount` | `entityCount(): number` | Direct count of live entities (cheaper than `liveEntities().length`). |
| `componentNames` | `componentNames(): readonly string[]` | Names of all components defined with `opts.name`, in registration order. Anonymous components are not listed. |
| `componentsOf` | `componentsOf(entity): ReadonlyArray<{ name: string; value: unknown }>` | The **named** components currently on an entity, paired with live values. Anonymous components omitted; a dead/unknown entity yields `[]`. |
| `componentByName` | `componentByName(name): Component<Record<string, unknown>> \| undefined` | Resolve a name (set via `opts.name`) to its callable token, **widened** to `Component<Record<string, unknown>>` so callers pass partial values without per-component generics or inline casts. Returns `undefined` for an unknown or anonymous name. On duplicate names, the **first registered** token wins (prefer unique names). |

```ts
const Position = app.ecs.defineComponent(() => ({ x: 0, y: 0 }), { name: "Position" });
app.ecs.componentNames();                  // ["Position", ...]
const token = app.ecs.componentByName("Position");  // same token reference, widened
if (token) app.ecs.add(entity, token, { x: 5 });    // use with add/set/get/has/remove
app.ecs.componentByName("Nope");           // undefined
```

### World resources (typed singletons) — first-class systems access

World resources are typed singletons attached to the world: shared services and state that systems
reach **through the `world` argument** (`(world, dt)`), with no closure capture. Each resource is
identified by an opaque, stably-keyed `Resource<T>` token (a sibling to `Component<T>`). The system
signature is unchanged — this is non-breaking.

Internally the world owns two private maps: `resourceValues` (set values, keyed by `Resource.__key`)
and `resourceFactories` (lazy-init factories registered by `defineResource(create)`). `defineResource`
mints monotonic `"res:N"` keys. Framework well-known tokens use reserved fixed-key prefixes that
never collide: the `context` plugin's `Assets` (`"ctx:assets"`) / `GameContext` (`"ctx:game"`) and
the `loop` plugin's `Time` (`"loop:time"`).

**Resource ops are IMMEDIATE — never command-buffered.** Unlike `spawn`/`despawn`/`add`/`remove`
(which defer during iteration), all resource methods apply synchronously even inside `updateEach`
or a system, because they touch only the resource maps, never archetype layout — so they cannot
corrupt an in-flight query. Resource ops are **not** counted by `maxStructuralOpsWarn`.

| Method | Signature | Semantics |
|---|---|---|
| `defineResource` | `defineResource<T>(create?: () => T): Resource<T>` | Mint a token with an auto-generated stable key (`"res:N"`). An optional `create` factory lazily initializes the value on first read (memoized thereafter); it stores **no** value at define time. Call once at module/setup scope. |
| `setResource` | `setResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>, value: T): void` | Store or replace the value. Applies immediately, even during iteration. |
| `getResource` | `getResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T \| undefined` | Read the value. Lazily initializes from the factory if one was registered and the value is unset (memoized); returns `undefined` if neither a value nor a factory exists. |
| `resource` | `resource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T` | Read the value, asserting presence — same lazy-init as `getResource`, but **throws** an actionable error instead of returning `undefined` when the resource is unset and has no factory. |
| `hasResource` | `hasResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): boolean` | `true` if a read would succeed — a value is set, **or** a default factory exists. |
| `removeResource` | `removeResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): void` | Clear the stored value. A factory, if any, re-initializes it on the next read. Immediate, never buffered. |

The thrown `resource()` error is:

```
[game] world.resource() — resource "<key>" is not set.
  Set it with world.setResource(token, value), or define it with a factory:
  world.defineResource(() => …). Framework resources (Assets, GameContext, Time) are wired at app.start().
```

```ts
const Score = app.ecs.defineResource(() => ({ value: 0, combo: 1 }));
app.ecs.setResource(Score, { value: 100, combo: 3 });   // immediate, anywhere
app.ecs.addSystem("update", (w) => {
  const score = w.resource(Score);     // asserts present → { value, combo }
  if (score.combo > 5) score.value *= 2;
  const maybe = w.getResource(Score);  // { value, combo } | undefined
  if (maybe) maybe.value += 10;
});
```

### Example

```ts
const Position = app.ecs.defineComponent(() => ({ x: 0, y: 0 }), { name: "Position" });
const Velocity = app.ecs.defineComponent(() => ({ dx: 0, dy: 0 }), { storage: "sparse" });
const Dead = app.ecs.defineTag();

const e = app.ecs.spawn(Position({ x: 0, y: 0 }), Velocity({ dx: 2, dy: 1 }));
app.ecs.set(e, Position, { x: 20 });
const pos = app.ecs.get(e, Position);   // { x: 20, y: 0 } | undefined

app.ecs.addSystem("update", (w, dt) => {
  w.query(Position, Velocity).updateEach(([p, v]) => { p.x += v.dx * dt; p.y += v.dy * dt; });
});
for (const entity of app.ecs.query(Dead)) app.ecs.despawn(entity);
app.ecs.tick(1 / 60);   // normally driven by loop → scheduler
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 5. scheduler — the ordered stage contract (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A thin, typed facade over the ECS world's stage/system registry. Stores no state of its own
(`State = Record<never, never>`); the registry lives entirely in the ecs world. No lifecycle hooks.

### Public types (src/plugins/scheduler/types.ts)

```ts
export type Config = { strictStages: boolean }; // @default true
export type Api = {
  readonly stages: readonly Stage[];                          // ["input","update","physics","sync","render"]
  addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void;        // forwards to world.addSystem
  tick(dt: number): void;                                     // forwards to world.tick
};
export type { Stage, System, World } from "../ecs/types";
```

### Stages

| Order | Stage | Typical use |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `input` | Apply queued input + externally-enqueued mutations (the mcp plugin drains its pending mutations here). |
| 2 | `update` | Game logic / gameplay systems. |
| 3 | `physics` | Movement and physics integration. |
| 4 | `sync` | Reconcile simulation state into render-facing data (the renderer's Transform→Pixi sync runs here). |
| 5 | `render` | Renderer-facing systems. |

The command buffer is flushed between stages, so structural ops queued in one stage are visible to
the next.

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `strictStages` | `boolean` | `true` | `true` → `addSystem` with an unknown stage throws an `Error` listing valid stages. `false` → logs a warning via `ctx.log.warn`, registers nothing, returns a no-op unsubscribe. |

`addSystem(stage, system)` returns an unsubscribe function. The ecs world is resolved lazily inside
`addSystem`/`tick` via `ctx.require(ecsPlugin)`.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 6. renderer — PixiJS v8 backend (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Isolates all of PixiJS v8 behind one plugin. Creates the Pixi `Application` asynchronously in
`onStart` with `autoStart: false` (Pixi's own ticker never fires — the loop drives every frame),
registers a `sync`-stage system mirroring ECS `Transform` into Pixi display objects, and destroys
the `Application` in `onStop` to guard against Pixi v8 VRAM leaks. Pixi types never cross the plugin
boundary except `HTMLCanvasElement` (getView) and `Container` (attach/getStage). `attachPrimitive`,
`screenshot`, and `tree` exchange **plain data only** (PrimitiveSpec / base64 string / SceneNode) —
no Pixi types leak.

### Public types (src/plugins/renderer/types.ts)

```ts
export type TransformValue = { x: number; y: number; rotation: number; scaleX: number; scaleY: number };

// JSON-serialisable snapshot of one Pixi display object (returned by tree()).
export type SceneNode = {
  label: string;        // the display object's `label`, or "" when unlabelled
  type: string;         // best-effort kind: "Text" | "Graphics" | "Sprite" | "Container"
  x: number; y: number; rotation: number; scaleX: number; scaleY: number;
  visible: boolean; alpha: number; width: number; height: number;
  text?: string;        // present only for Pixi Text nodes
  children: SceneNode[];
};

// Shared style fields for every PrimitiveSpec shape. Plain data — hex-int colors, no Pixi types.
export type PrimitiveStyle = {
  fill?: number;         // fill color as a hex int (e.g. 0xff0000), or undefined for no fill
  stroke?: number;       // stroke color as a hex int, or undefined for no stroke
  strokeWidth?: number;  // stroke width in px; default 1 when a stroke color is set
  alpha?: number;        // node opacity 0–1; default 1
  label?: string;        // Pixi node label so tree() can report it
};

// JSON-describable primitive shape + style, built into a Pixi Graphics by attachPrimitive.
// Discriminated union over `shape`; no Pixi types leak.
export type PrimitiveSpec =
  | ({ shape: "rect"; width: number; height: number } & PrimitiveStyle)
  | ({ shape: "circle"; radius: number } & PrimitiveStyle)
  | ({ shape: "line"; x2: number; y2: number } & PrimitiveStyle)
  | ({ shape: "polygon"; points: ReadonlyArray<{ x: number; y: number }> } & PrimitiveStyle);
// Anchor contract: `rect` & `circle` are drawn CENTERED on the entity's Transform; `polygon` points are
// in the entity's local space with the origin at the Transform; `line` runs from the local origin to (x2, y2).

export type Config = {
  width: number;       // @default 800
  height: number;      // @default 600
  background: number;  // @default 0x000000
  resolution: number;  // @default 0 (→ window.devicePixelRatio, then 1 headless)
  antialias: boolean;  // @default true
  mount: string | undefined; // @default undefined (manual mounting via getView())
  headless: boolean;   // @default detectHeadless() (true when typeof document === "undefined")
};

export type Api = {
  readonly Transform: Component<TransformValue>;  // throws if read before app.start()
  attach(entity: Entity, view: Container): void;  // Container/Sprite/Graphics; marks dirty — caller owns stage.addChild
  attachPrimitive(entity: Entity, spec: PrimitiveSpec): boolean;  // builds a Graphics, ADDS it to the stage itself, registers for sync; false when headless/before start
  detach(entity: Entity): void;                   // destroy()s view; idempotent
  render(): void;                                 // draws one frame; no-op before start
  screenshot(): Promise<string | undefined>;      // PNG data URL via Pixi extract.base64; undefined when headless/before start
  tree(): SceneNode | undefined;                  // JSON scene-graph snapshot rooted at the stage; undefined when headless/before start
  getView(): HTMLCanvasElement | undefined;       // canvas for manual mounting
  getStage(): Container | undefined;              // root Pixi stage
  getEntityView(entity: Entity): Container | undefined;  // entity's attached view (undefined if none/headless); view-local state (tint/alpha) the Transform sync doesn't manage — e.g. vfx flash
  markDirty(entity: Entity): void;                // re-sync view on next "sync" tick
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `width` | `number` | `800` | Canvas width in CSS pixels. |
| `height` | `number` | `600` | Canvas height in CSS pixels. |
| `background` | `number` | `0x000000` | Background fill color (hex, e.g. `0x1099bb`). |
| `resolution` | `number` | `0` | Device-pixel-ratio resolution; `0` falls back to `window.devicePixelRatio` (then `1` headless). |
| `antialias` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable antialiasing. |
| `mount` | `string \| undefined` | `undefined` | CSS selector to auto-mount the canvas on start. `undefined` = manual mounting via `getView()`. |
| `headless` | `boolean` | `detectHeadless()` → `true` when `typeof document === "undefined"`, else `false` (explicit value overrides) | Run without Pixi/GPU. `onStart` skips `Application` creation/init, leaves `state.app` undefined, and `render()`/`getView()`/`getStage()`/`screenshot()`/`tree()`/`attachPrimitive()` are no-ops (returning `undefined`/`false`) — but `Transform` is still defined and `sync` still registered, so ECS/scene code is identical in both modes. |

### attach vs attachPrimitive

- **`attach(entity, view)`** — registers a Pixi `Container` (or subclass) you constructed and added
  to the stage yourself. The renderer only tracks + repositions it from `Transform` each `sync`
  tick; it does **not** call `stage.addChild` for you.
- **`attachPrimitive(entity, spec)`** — takes a plain-data `PrimitiveSpec`, builds a Pixi `Graphics`
  from it, **adds it to the stage itself**, and registers it (views + dirty) so the sync system
  positions it from the entity's `Transform`. Designed so an MCP-spawned entity actually renders
  without the caller needing a stage handle. Returns `false` when headless / before start (nothing
  is added). Backed by `src/plugins/renderer/primitives.ts`.

### tree() / SceneNode

`tree()` returns a depth-capped, JSON-serialisable snapshot of the Pixi scene graph rooted at the
stage — the most direct way for an agent to read on-screen positions and text. `SceneNode.type`
reports a best-effort kind from the set `"Text" | "Graphics" | "Sprite" | "Container"` (Graphics
nodes — including those from `attachPrimitive` — now report `"Graphics"` correctly). `text` is
present only for Pixi `Text` nodes.

### Lifecycle & design

- **`detectHeadless()`:** a pure env probe (`typeof document === "undefined"`) used as the `headless`
  config default, computed at module load in `index.ts`. It does NOT import or touch Pixi. An
  explicit `headless` value always overrides it.
- **`onStart` (headless path):** logs, skips Pixi entirely (no `new Application()`, no `init`),
  defines the `Transform` component and registers the `sync` system, then stores
  `{ app: undefined, views }` in the `WeakMap`.
- **`onStart` (non-headless path):** `new Application()` → `await app.init({ width, height,
  background, antialias, autoStart: false, resolution })`. On reject, `app.destroy(...)` then rethrow
  (no half-open GPU context). On success: optionally append `app.canvas` to
  `document.querySelector(config.mount)`; defines `Transform` + registers the sync system; writes
  `app` into state; stashes `{ app, views }` in a module-level `WeakMap` keyed on `ctx.global`.
- **`onStop`:** reads `{ app, views }` from the `WeakMap`, `destroy()`s every managed `Container`,
  then — **only when `app` is present** (non-headless) — `app.destroy(true, { children: true,
  texture: true, textureSource: true })` for full VRAM cleanup, and deletes the `WeakMap` entry.
  Idempotent.
- **Dirty-flag sync:** `attach`/`attachPrimitive`/`markDirty` add the entity to `state.dirty`; each
  `sync` tick repositions only dirty views (`container.position`, `rotation`, `scale` from
  `Transform`) then clears the set. A second pass diffs `state.views` against `world.isAlive(...)` to
  dispose views of despawned entities.
- Runtime package: `pixi.js@^8`. Composes with `@moku-labs/web` for DOM mounting when `mount` is a
  selector.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 7. input — polled keyboard/pointer + injection (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A **polled** model, not event-driven — nothing about input flows through the kernel event bus. DOM
listeners mutate live edge-sets; once per frame an `"input"`-stage system freezes them into an
immutable `InputSnapshot` and clears the edge sets, so every later system in the tick reads a
consistent, frame-aligned view. `justPressed`/`justReleased` are true for exactly one frame.
Programmatic `keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress` let an agent or test drive gameplay without DOM events.

### Public types (src/plugins/input/types.ts)

```ts
export type InputSnapshot = {
  isDown(key: string): boolean;        // held (key matches KeyboardEvent.key, e.g. "ArrowRight", " ", "a")
  justPressed(key: string): boolean;   // one frame only; key-repeat ignored
  justReleased(key: string): boolean;  // one frame only
  readonly pointer: { readonly x: number; readonly y: number; readonly buttons: number };
};

export type Config = {
  target: string;          // @default "window" ("window" → global; else CSS selector via querySelector)
  pointer: boolean;        // @default true
  keyboard: boolean;       // @default true
  preventDefault: boolean; // @default false
};

export type Api = {
  snapshot(): InputSnapshot;
  keyDown(key: string): void;   // inject a held key (alias-normalized)
  keyUp(key: string): void;     // inject a release (alias-normalized)
  keyPress(key: string): void;  // inject a one-frame tap: justPressed + justReleased, never sticks (alias-normalized)
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `target` | `string` | `"window"` | DOM target for listeners. `"window"` = global window; any other string is a CSS selector resolved via `document.querySelector` at start. |
| `pointer` | `boolean` | `true` | Attach `pointermove`/`pointerdown`/`pointerup`; track position + buttons. |
| `keyboard` | `boolean` | `true` | Attach `keydown`/`keyup`; track key state. |
| `preventDefault` | `boolean` | `false` | Call `event.preventDefault()` on tracked key events (e.g. stop arrow/space scrolling). |

### Key injection + alias normalization (Cycle 5)

`keyDown`/`keyUp`/`keyPress` write the input edge-sets directly — **immediately, between frames, NOT
command-buffered** — exactly as a real DOM event would, so the next input-stage snapshot observes
them. Each method first **normalizes** its `key` argument through a frozen alias map before touching
the edge-sets, so injected keys agree with the canonical `KeyboardEvent.key` the DOM handler stores:

| Alias | Canonical `KeyboardEvent.key` |
|---|---|
| `"Space"` | `" "` (a single space) |
| `"Spacebar"` | `" "` |
| `"Esc"` | `"Escape"` |

All other keys (arrow keys, letters, digits, already-canonical `" "` / `"Escape"`) pass through
unchanged. So `app.input.keyDown("Space")` is equivalent to `keyDown(" ")`, and `keyPress("Esc")` is
equivalent to `keyPress("Escape")`. The snapshot taken after injection therefore observes the
**canonical** key, not the alias: after `keyDown("Space")` the next snapshot reports `isDown(" ")
=== true` (not `isDown("Space")`). Query the snapshot with the same canonical strings the DOM
handler uses. (`normalizeKey` is exported for unit testing only and is **not** part of the public
`Api`.) `keyDown` only records the just-pressed edge when the key was not already down (OS key-repeat
does not re-fire `justPressed`); `keyPress` adds both edges and never leaves the key in the held set.

`pointer.buttons` is a bitmask: `1` = left, `2` = right, `4` = middle. `snapshot()` is stable for the
whole tick. Before the first tick, `snapshot()` returns an initial snapshot backed by empty sets.
Lifecycle attaches/detaches DOM listeners (`onStart`/`onStop`), tracked through a module-level
`WeakMap` keyed on `ctx.global`. Target resolution is node-safe; falls back to `globalThis`
headless. Depends only on `scheduler` (which already depends on ecs).

### Example

```ts
app.scheduler.addSystem("update", (world, dt) => {
  const input = app.input.snapshot();
  if (input.isDown("ArrowLeft")) player.x -= player.speed * dt;
  if (input.justPressed(" ")) player.jump();          // canonical spacebar key
  const { x, y, buttons } = input.pointer;
  if (buttons & 1) player.aimAt(x, y);
});

// Drive gameplay programmatically (agent / test):
app.input.keyDown("ArrowRight");   // hold right (canonical "ArrowRight")
app.input.keyPress("Space");       // one-frame jump → snapshot sees justPressed(" ")
app.input.keyUp("ArrowRight");     // stop holding right
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 8. loop — fixed-timestep rAF loop + Time resource (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Owns the frame. The framework's **kernel-bypassing hot path**: Moku Core has no built-in per-frame
tick, so the loop drives `scheduler.tick` → `world.tick` and `renderer.render` directly, with no
event-bus dispatch on the per-frame path. It also publishes the **`Time`** ECS world resource — the
canonical frame clock — and is its sole writer.

### Public types (src/plugins/loop/types.ts)

```ts
import type { Resource } from "../ecs/types";

// Frame clock published as the Time world resource. Consumer-facing fields are readonly;
// the loop mutates the backing object in place each fixed step — no per-step allocation.
export type TimeState = {
  readonly dt: number;       // fixed step length, in SECONDS (always == config.fixedDt)
  readonly elapsed: number;  // total simulated time since start, in SECONDS (sum of all fixed steps)
  readonly frame: number;    // count of fixed steps simulated since start (a count, not seconds)
};

// Value returned by step(): a snapshot of the just-advanced clock.
// Equivalent to Pick<TimeState, "frame" | "elapsed" | "dt">.
export type TimeStepResult = Pick<TimeState, "frame" | "elapsed" | "dt">;

export type Config = {
  fixedDt: number;          // @default 1/60
  maxFrameDelta: number;    // @default 0.25
  maxStepsPerFrame: number; // @default 5
  autoStart: boolean;       // @default true
};

export type Api = {
  start(): void;        // begin/resume rAF loop (no-op if running); resets accumulator + lastTime
  stop(): void;         // pause; cancelAnimationFrame, running = false (no-op if not running)
  isRunning(): boolean;
  step(): TimeStepResult;  // exactly one fixed tick + render, NO real-time accumulation; also advances Time; returns the clock snapshot
  readonly time: Resource<TimeState>;  // well-known Time token; also exported as `Time` from loop/resources
};
```

### `step()` return value (Cycle 5)

`step()` previously returned `void`; it now returns a `TimeStepResult` snapshot of the clock taken
immediately after it advances `Time` and calls `render()` — `{ frame, elapsed, dt }`, the values
visible to systems during that step. After one step from zero it returns `{ frame: 1, elapsed:
0.016…, dt: 0.016… }`. A **no-runtime** call (before `start` / after `stop`, when there is no loop
runtime) returns `{ frame: 0, elapsed: 0, dt: 0 }`. Widening from `void` to an object is
non-breaking — existing void-context callers are unaffected. The `mcp` `loop:step` tool returns this
snapshot to the agent as `{ stepped: true, frame, elapsed, dt }`.

### Fixed-timestep model

Each rAF frame: (1) compute real delta in seconds (first frame only seeds `lastTime`, skips
ticking); (2) clamp delta to `maxFrameDelta` (spiral-of-death guard); (3) add to `accumulator`, run
`scheduler.tick(fixedDt)` while `accumulator >= fixedDt`, up to `maxStepsPerFrame` times (each step
subtracts `fixedDt`; leftover carries forward); (4) `renderer.render()` once; (5) re-schedule via
`requestAnimationFrame` unless `stop()` cleared `running`. A `visibilitychange` listener resets
`accumulator` + `lastTime` on tab return so a backgrounded tab does not produce a catch-up burst.

### The `Time` world resource

The loop is the **sole writer of frame time**, so it publishes a first-class `Time` ECS world
resource. ECS systems have signature `(world, dt)`; the `dt` argument is the fixed step, but a system
that needs the running total or frame count reads them from `Time` via `world.resource(Time)` rather
than threading extra arguments. `Time` lives in the loop (not `context`) so a single owner avoids two
plugins racing on one clock object.

- **Binding:** on `onStart` the loop calls `world.setResource(Time, { dt: 0, elapsed: 0, frame: 0 })`
  and caches that same object on its per-instance `LoopRuntime`. Each fixed step it mutates the cached
  object **in place** immediately before `scheduler.tick(fixedDt)` — so a system running that step
  already sees the updated clock. Because the world's registry holds the same reference,
  `world.resource(Time)` reflects every update with **no per-frame allocation**.
- **Per-step update** (in both the rAF callback and `step()`):

```ts
runtime.time.dt = config.fixedDt;       // current step length (seconds)
runtime.time.elapsed += config.fixedDt; // running total (seconds)
runtime.time.frame += 1;                // step counter
```

- **Two ways to reach the token** (both the same `Resource<TimeState>`):
  `import { Time } from "../loop/resources"` (module-level const, `__key: "loop:time"`), or
  `app.loop.time` (surfaced on the public API for consumer code).
- **Teardown:** `Time` needs no explicit teardown — its value is released with the ECS world on stop.

```ts
import { Time } from "../loop/resources";
import type { World } from "../ecs/types";

app.scheduler.addSystem("update", (world: World, dt: number) => {
  const t = world.resource(Time);   // → { dt, elapsed, frame }
  if (t.frame % 60 === 0) console.log(`elapsed ${t.elapsed.toFixed(2)}s (frame ${t.frame})`);
});

const { frame, elapsed, dt } = app.loop.step();  // deterministic single step → { frame, elapsed, dt }
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fixedDt` | `number` | `1 / 60` | Fixed simulation step in seconds; each `scheduler.tick` receives this. |
| `maxFrameDelta` | `number` | `0.25` | Max real delta (s) consumed per frame before clamping. |
| `maxStepsPerFrame` | `number` | `5` | Hard cap on fixed steps per frame after clamping. |
| `autoStart` | `boolean` | `true` | Schedule the first frame in `onStart`; `false` → call `app.loop.start()`. |

`start`/`stop` double as resume/pause (mcp `loop:resume`/`loop:pause`). `step()` is what the mcp
`loop:step` tool drives — deterministic, works whether running or stopped, advances `Time` exactly
as a real frame would, and returns its `TimeStepResult` snapshot. Lifecycle: rAF id +
`visibilitychange` listener live in a module-level `WeakMap` keyed on `ctx.global`; `onStart` also
binds the `Time` resource; `onStop` cancels the pending rAF and removes the listener. **Dependencies:
scheduler, renderer, ecs** (`ecs` needed to call `world.setResource(Time, …)`; no cycle). No package
dependencies beyond `@moku-labs/core`.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 9. assets — Pixi v8 Assets wrapper (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Loads and caches textures/spritesheets by alias, builds `Sprite`s, emits `assets:loaded`. The
renderer must boot first (`depends: [rendererPlugin]`); each method lazily calls
`ctx.require(rendererPlugin)` to guarantee the Pixi `Application` (and shared Assets/GPU context) is
running. No `onStart`/`onStop` — it borrows the renderer's Application rather than owning a resource.

### Public types (src/plugins/assets/types.ts)

```ts
export type Events = { "assets:loaded": { alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" } };

export type Config = {
  basePath: string;                          // @default ""
  manifest: Readonly<Record<string, string>>; // @default {}
  throwOnError: boolean;                     // @default true
};

export type Api = {
  load(alias: string): Promise<Texture>;
  loadBundle(bundle: string, entries: Readonly<Record<string, string>>): Promise<Record<string, Texture>>;
  get(alias: string): Texture | undefined;   // cache read; does NOT load
  sprite(alias: string): Promise<Sprite>;    // load-on-miss, then build Sprite
  isLoaded(alias: string): boolean;          // session state.loaded, not the Pixi cache
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `basePath` | `string` | `""` | Base URL/path prepended to every resolved URL. |
| `manifest` | `Readonly<Record<string, string>>` | `{}` | alias → URL; aliases here are preferred over the raw alias string. |
| `throwOnError` | `boolean` | `true` | `true` → rethrow load errors. `false` → log via `ctx.log.error` and `load()` resolves `undefined` (typed `Texture` for ergonomics — do not rely on the value). |

### URL resolution

For each alias: if `manifest[alias]` exists → use it; else → treat the alias as the URL. `basePath`
is prepended when non-empty. E.g. with `basePath: "assets/"`, `manifest: { ship: "sprites/ship.png"
}`: `load("ship")` → `Assets.load("assets/sprites/ship.png")`; `load("tank.png")` →
`Assets.load("assets/tank.png")`.

`assets:loaded` is coarse — emitted once per `load()`/`loadBundle()` call, never per texture. A
bundle of N textures emits exactly one event keyed on the bundle name. `sprite()` checks `get(alias)`
first and only loads on a cache miss (a repeat call does not re-emit).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 10. context — well-known world resources (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ECS systems have signature `(world, dt)` — they never see the plugin `ctx`. The `context` plugin
closes that gap: at framework start it binds two **well-known world resources** onto the ECS world,
so any system can reach framework services via `world.resource(token)` with no closure over `app`.
It owns no per-frame logic and stores no domain data (`State = Record<never, never>`) — it is a thin
wiring + facade plugin sitting **above** both `ecs` and `assets`.

1. **`Assets`** — the `assets` plugin API (`load`/`loadBundle`/`get`/`sprite`/`isLoaded`). **Always**
   bound, regardless of config.
2. **`GameContext`** — a curated, hot-path-safe facade `{ log, emit, env }` built from this plugin's
   own `ctx`. Bound only when `config.bindGameContext` is `true`.

### Public types (src/plugins/context/types.ts, resources.ts)

```ts
import type { EnvApi, LogApi } from "@moku-labs/common";
import type { EmitFn } from "@moku-labs/core";
import type { Events as FrameworkEvents } from "../../config";
import type { Api as AssetsApi } from "../assets/types";
import type { Resource } from "../ecs/types";

export type Config = {
  bindGameContext: boolean;   // @default true
};

export type State = Record<never, never>;  // no per-instance state; values live in the ecs world

// Curated, hot-path-safe game context exposed to systems via the GameContext resource.
export type GameContextValue = {
  readonly log: LogApi;                      // ctx.log from the common logPlugin
  readonly emit: EmitFn<FrameworkEvents>;    // emit a coarse framework event (assets:loaded | scene:loaded)
  readonly env: EnvApi;                      // ctx.env from the common envPlugin
};

// API — the well-known resource tokens (static; valid before start).
export type Api = {
  readonly assets: Resource<AssetsApi>;       // token; read in a system: world.resource(assets)
  readonly game: Resource<GameContextValue>;  // token; read in a system: world.resource(game)
};

// resources.ts — fixed-key module consts:
export const Assets: Resource<AssetsApi> = { __key: "ctx:assets" };
export const GameContext: Resource<GameContextValue> = { __key: "ctx:game" };
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bindGameContext` | `boolean` | `true` | When `true`, bind the curated `GameContext` resource (`{ log, emit, env }`) at start. `Assets` is bound either way. Disable only if a consumer wants to provide its own game-context resource. |

### Lifecycle & design

- **`onStart`-only:** `start(ctx)` does three things: (1) `const world = ctx.require(ecsPlugin)`;
  (2) `world.setResource(Assets, ctx.require(assetsPlugin))` — **always**; (3) if
  `ctx.config.bindGameContext`, `world.setResource(GameContext, { log: ctx.log, emit: ctx.emit, env: ctx.env })`.
  No `onInit`, no `onStop` (the bindings are released with the world).
- **Static API, fixed-key tokens:** `createApi` returns the two token consts (`{ assets: Assets,
  game: GameContext }`). The tokens are plain module consts (fixed keys `"ctx:assets"` /
  `"ctx:game"`), valid to reference/capture immediately — only their *values* are unset until
  `start`. `app.context.assets` is the same object as the exported `Assets`; `app.context.game` is
  the same as `GameContext`.
- **Curated `GameContext`:** exactly `{ log, emit, env }` — `log`/`env` are the *same references* as
  `ctx.log`/`ctx.env`; `emit` forwards `ctx.emit`. It deliberately **excludes** kernel escape
  hatches (`require`/`has`/`global`). Frame time is delivered separately as the loop's `Time`
  resource, not here.
- **`depends: [ecs, assets]` only:** the global `Events` are visible to every plugin's `ctx.emit`,
  so `scene:loaded` is emittable through `GameContext.emit` without depending on `scene`.
- **Ordering:** `context.onStart` runs after `assets.onStart` (start runs in dependency order), and
  the loop's first tick fires after `app.start()` has awaited every `onStart` — so
  `Assets`/`GameContext` are always bound before any system runs.

### Example

```ts
const { assets, game } = app.context;   // capture the well-known tokens once (like component tokens)

app.ecs.addSystem("render", (world, _dt) => {
  const a = world.resource(assets);   // AssetsApi — first-class, no `app` capture
  const g = world.resource(game);     // { log, emit, env }
  const ship = a.get("ship");
  if (!ship) g.log.warn("ship texture not loaded");
});
```

`world.resource(token)` throws the ecs "resource is not set" error if read **before** `start`. With
`bindGameContext: false`, `Assets` is still bound but reading `game` throws.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 11. scene — named scene lifecycle + ownership (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A scene is a registered `setup(world)` that spawns the entities for one screen/level. The crux is
**entity-ownership tracking**: during `setup`, the plugin hands a thin tracking wrapper around the
world whose `spawn` is intercepted — every spawned entity is recorded as owned. Transitioning (or
`unload`) detaches each owned entity's renderer view and despawns it, so nothing leaks between
scenes. If a scene declares a `bundle`, it is pre-loaded via the assets plugin before `setup` runs.
(The tracking wrapper delegates the world resource + introspection methods through to the real world,
so its public API is unchanged.)

### Public types (src/plugins/scene/types.ts)

```ts
import type { Entity, World } from "../ecs/types";

export type Events = { "scene:loaded": { name: string } };

export type SceneDefinition = {
  setup: (world: World) => void | Promise<void>;  // spawns entities; may be async
  bundle?: Readonly<Record<string, string>>;       // optional alias→url, pre-loaded before setup
};

export type Config = {
  initial: string | undefined; // @default undefined (RESERVED — not auto-loaded in this version)
  despawnOnUnload: boolean;    // @default true
};

export type Api = {
  define(name: string, definition: SceneDefinition): void;  // re-defining overwrites
  load(name: string): Promise<void>;                        // throws if name was never defined
  unload(): void;                                            // no-op if no scene; does NOT emit scene:loaded
  currentScene(): string | undefined;
  sceneNames(): readonly string[];      // all registered scene names in registration order; [] before any define
  ownedEntities(): readonly Entity[];   // snapshot of current scene's owned entity handles; [] after unload
};
```

### New read-only methods (Cycle 5)

| Method | Signature | Semantics |
|---|---|---|
| `sceneNames` | `sceneNames(): readonly string[]` | All registered scene names, in registration order (equivalent to `[...state.scenes.keys()]`). Returns `[]` before any `define`. |
| `ownedEntities` | `ownedEntities(): readonly Entity[]` | A fresh snapshot of the entity handles owned by the current scene (equivalent to `[...state.owned]`). Returns `[]` after `unload`. Mutating the returned array does NOT affect `state.owned`. |

Both back the `mcp` `scene:getInfo` tool and the `scene:load` name-validation. (`mcp` reads
`sceneNames()` to reject unknown scene names with an error listing the known ones.)

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `initial` | `string \| undefined` | `undefined` | Reserved; NOT auto-loaded in this version — call `app.scene.load(...)` after `app.start()`. |
| `despawnOnUnload` | `boolean` | `true` | `true` → owned entities detached + despawned on unload. `false` → ownership cleared and `current` reset, entities left alive. |

### `load(name)` ordered steps

1. **Unload current** — despawn the previous scene's owned entities + detach renderer views (subject
   to `despawnOnUnload`), reset `current`.
2. **Pre-load bundle** — if `definition.bundle` is set, `await assets.loadBundle(name, bundle)`.
3. **Run setup** — `await definition.setup(wrappedWorld)`; every `spawn` is tracked as owned.
4. **Commit + emit** — `current = name`, emit `scene:loaded` with `{ name }`.

Partial-spawn safety: entities are recorded as each `spawn` returns, so a `setup` that throws
part-way still has its spawned entities cleaned up on the next transition. Depends on ecs (world +
`despawn`), renderer (`detach`), assets (`loadBundle`). Lazy `ctx.require` inside methods.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 12. mcp — first-class MCP server (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Attaches a Model Context Protocol server to the running framework. Agent clients (Claude, Cursor,
etc.) query live state, control the loop, spawn/despawn entities with real components, mutate
components, attach visible primitives, inject keys, trigger scene loads, and capture screenshots —
without touching game code. Depends on its seven build-dependency plugins (ecs, scheduler, renderer,
input, loop, assets, scene — not the independent `audio`). The default transport is
**environment-aware** (`["inMemory"]` in a browser, `["stdio"]` under Node/Bun), so a default
`createApp().start()` runs in-page without a socket and never crashes on a missing `process.stdin`.

### Public types (src/plugins/mcp/types.ts)

```ts
export type Config = {
  transports: ReadonlyArray<"stdio" | "http" | "inMemory">; // @default env-aware: ["inMemory"] browser, ["stdio"] Node/Bun
  httpHost: string;                             // @default "127.0.0.1"
  httpPort: number;                             // @default 3333
  httpAuth: "none" | "bearer";                  // @default "none"
  bearerToken: string;                          // @default ""  (required + non-empty when httpAuth==="bearer")
  enableMutations: boolean;                     // @default true
  inMemoryGlobalKey: string;                    // @default "__MOKU_GAME_MCP__" ("" disables the globalThis publish)
};

// Plugin event: emitted after the game:reset tool's hard-reset closure runs.
export type Events = {
  "game:reset": { reason: "mcp" };
};

// Structural subset of the SDK Transport interface — no @modelcontextprotocol/sdk type leaks into the public surface.
export type InMemoryClientTransportLike = {
  start(): Promise<void>;
  send(message: unknown, options?: unknown): Promise<void>;
  close(): Promise<void>;
  onclose?: () => void;
  onerror?: (error: Error) => void;
  onmessage?: (message: unknown, extra?: unknown) => void;
  sessionId?: string;
};

export type Api = {
  isRunning(): boolean;                  // true when connected on ≥1 transport
  httpEndpoint(): string | undefined;    // e.g. "http://127.0.0.1:3333/mcp", or undefined when no "http" transport
  toolNames(): readonly string[];        // names of all registered tools (stable after onStart)
  clientTransport(): InMemoryClientTransportLike | undefined; // in-page client paired with the server when "inMemory" is active
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `transports` | `ReadonlyArray<"stdio" \| "http" \| "inMemory">` | env-aware: `["inMemory"]` in a browser, `["stdio"]` under Node/Bun | Which transports to expose. `"inMemory"` is in-page only (no socket). |
| `httpHost` | `string` | `"127.0.0.1"` | HTTP bind address (localhost only by default). |
| `httpPort` | `number` | `3333` | HTTP server port. |
| `httpAuth` | `"none" \| "bearer"` | `"none"` | `"none"` trusts the local network; `"bearer"` requires a token on every request. |
| `bearerToken` | `string` | `""` | Required (non-empty, validated at startup) when `httpAuth === "bearer"`. |
| `enableMutations` | `boolean` | `true` | `false` → only the four read-only tools are registered. |
| `inMemoryGlobalKey` | `string` | `"__MOKU_GAME_MCP__"` | `globalThis` property name on which the in-page client transport is published when `"inMemory"` is active in a browser. `""` disables the publish (`clientTransport()` still works). |

### MCP tool catalog — 15 tools

Read-only (always registered — 4):

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `ecs:query` | Query ALL live entities (via the ecs introspection facet), optionally filtered to those having every named component in `componentNames` (empty/omitted = all). Returns each entity's id + named components with values, plus `count`. Unknown filter names return an error listing the known component names. |
| `renderer:screenshot` | Current frame as raw base64 PNG (data-URI prefix stripped) via Pixi `extract.base64` (reliable while paused). Error result when headless / not started. |
| `renderer:tree` | The Pixi scene-graph snapshot (`SceneNode`) rooted at the stage — labels, positions, rotation/scale, visibility, `text` for Text nodes; `type` ∈ Container/Sprite/Text/Graphics. Error result when headless / not started. |
| `scene:getInfo` | `{ current, scenes, owned }` — current scene name, all registered scene names (`sceneNames()`), and the current scene's owned entity handles (`ownedEntities()`). |

Mutating / play (registered when `enableMutations: true` — 11):

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `ecs:spawn` | Spawn an entity with **optional named components** (`components`: map of name → partial value). Components are resolved via `componentByName` and applied with `world.add`. ALL names must be known — any unknown name returns an error (listing known names) and aborts before creating any entity. Returns `{ entity }` (and `components` when supplied); adds the entity to the MCP-tracked set. |
| `ecs:despawn` | Despawn an entity by id. Returns `{ despawned: id, changed }` — `changed` is `false` when the entity was already dead. Removes it from the tracked set. |
| `ecs:setComponent` | **Upsert** a component value on an entity: `set` (shallow-merge) if present, else `add` (value merged over defaults). Requires the component to have been defined with `opts.name`. Unknown name or dead entity → `isError` (validated before enqueue). Returns `{ id, component, changed: true, value }`. |
| `ecs:removeComponent` | Remove a named component from an entity. Returns `{ id, component, changed }` — `false` when the component was not present. Unknown name or dead entity → `isError`. |
| `renderer:attach` | Attach a primitive shape (rect/circle/line/polygon, validated by a zod `PrimitiveSpec` schema) to an entity via `renderer.attachPrimitive` — builds a Pixi Graphics and adds it to the stage so the sync system positions it from `Transform`. Dead entity → `isError`; headless / before start → error (`attached` was `false`). Returns `{ id, attached: true }`. |
| `input:key` | Inject a key so an agent can play: `action` `"down"` holds, `"up"` releases, `"press"` is a one-frame tap. Applied immediately between frames (the input plugin normalizes `"Space"` → `" "` etc.). Returns `{ key, action }`. |
| `loop:step` | Advance the loop one fixed step + render (deterministic). Returns the clock snapshot `{ stepped: true, frame, elapsed, dt }` from `loop.step()`. |
| `loop:pause` | Pause the rAF loop (`loop.stop()`). Returns `{ paused: true }`. |
| `loop:resume` | Resume the rAF loop (`loop.start()`). Returns `{ resumed: true }`. |
| `scene:load` | Load a named scene (unloads current first). Validates the name against `sceneNames()` — unknown name returns an error listing known scenes. The load is fire-and-forget (scheduled on the next tick); returns `{ scheduled: name }`. Poll `game://scene/current` for completion. |
| `game:reset` | Hard reset: despawn all MCP-tracked entities, clear the tracked set, call `scene.unload()`, then **emit the `game:reset` event** (`{ reason: "mcp" }`). Deferred to the next input-stage tick. Returns `{ reset: true }`. |

### MCP resource catalog (4 read-only resources)

| URI | Description |
|---|---|
| `game://world/snapshot` | JSON `{ entities, count }` — **every** live entity (via `liveEntities()`) with its named component values (`componentsOf`). |
| `game://systems/list` | JSON `{ stages }` — registered scheduler **stage names only**; systems are anonymous functions, so per-stage system names/counts are not tracked. |
| `game://stats/frame` | JSON `{ frame, lastDt, entityCount }` — current frame number, last delta (s), live entity count (all entities in the world). |
| `game://scene/current` | JSON `{ current }` — the current scene name, or `undefined` when none is loaded. |

### Design notes

- **Frame-safe mutations:** mutating tools push closures into a `pending: Array<() => void>` queue
  drained on each `"input"` stage tick by a registered ECS system, via `enqueueMutation` (the closure
  is awaited and its return value resolved to the caller). `input:key` is the exception — it injects
  directly between frames (NOT command-buffered) so the input-stage snapshot observes correct edge
  timing. Read-only tools and loop controls run directly between frames.
- **Honest results (Cycle 5):** unknown component names (`componentByName` miss), dead/non-live
  entities (`isAlive` false), and unknown scene names (`sceneNames()` miss) return MCP `isError`
  (error text lists the known names) instead of optimistic success; `ecs:despawn` /
  `ecs:removeComponent` report `{ changed: boolean }` honestly. Validation runs **before**
  enqueueing the mutation.
- **`ecs:spawn` spawns REAL entities with components** resolved via `componentByName` (no longer a
  bare-entity stub). `ecs:setComponent` / `ecs:removeComponent` are the real ECS mutation triad
  alongside it (no longer v1 no-ops).
- **Environment-aware default + `inMemory` transport:** `defaultTransports()` (a pure env probe in
  `transport.ts`, no SDK call) picks `["inMemory"]` in a browser and `["stdio"]` under Node/Bun. The
  `inMemory` transport is built on the SDK's `InMemoryTransport.createLinkedPair()` — the server side
  is connected, the client side is retained on the handle and returned by `clientTransport()`. When
  `"inMemory"` is active in a browser and `inMemoryGlobalKey` is non-empty, the client transport is
  also published on `globalThis[inMemoryGlobalKey]` (removed on `onStop`).
- **stdio guard:** before constructing the stdio transport the plugin checks `typeof process !==
  "undefined" && process.stdin`; in a browser it skips stdio and emits a `ctx.log.warn` instead of
  letting the SDK throw an opaque `reading 'on'` error.
- **Tracked entities:** the `trackedEntities` Set scopes **mutation cleanup** (`ecs:despawn`,
  `game:reset`) to MCP-created entities; reads (`ecs:query`, `game://world/snapshot`) enumerate the
  whole world via the ecs introspection facet.
- **SDK isolation:** all `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` imports are confined to `transport.ts` (incl.
  `InMemoryTransport`); `clientTransport()` returns the SDK-free structural
  `InMemoryClientTransportLike`. `zod` is imported in `tools.ts` for input schemas (a schema lib, not
  the SDK — allowed by the seam rule), including the `PrimitiveSpec` discriminated-union schema.
- **Tool naming:** tools use `domain:action` (`ecs:spawn`, `loop:step`). The MCP SDK warns `:` is
  non-standard; registration still proceeds, but strict clients may need a name-mapping adapter.
- **Bearer auth:** when `httpAuth: "bearer"`, the HTTP handler compares the `Authorization` header in
  constant time (`node:crypto` `timingSafeEqual`).
- **WeakMap pattern:** per-instance `McpHandle` lives in a module-level `WeakMap` keyed on
  `ctx.global`, mirroring the loop plugin.

Runtime packages: `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `hono` (HTTP transport), `zod` (tool input schemas).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 13. audio — native WebAudio SFX + music (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Native WebAudio SFX + music with **zero runtime dependencies** (no Howler, no Pixi). Owns a small
gain graph directly; foundational (Wave 1), depends on no game plugin.

### Public API (src/plugins/audio/types.ts) — `app.audio`

```ts
type Channel = "master" | "sfx" | "music";

type Api = {
  unlock(): Promise<void>;                          // resume AudioContext after a user gesture; idempotent
  load(name: string, url?: string): Promise<void>;  // fetch + decodeAudioData + cache; url falls back to manifest[name]
  play(name: string, opts?: { volume?: number; rate?: number }): void;        // one-shot on the sfx channel
  playMusic(name: string, opts?: { loop?: boolean; fadeIn?: number }): void;   // looping on the music channel; stops previous
  stopMusic(opts?: { fadeOut?: number }): void;
  mute(): void;                                     // zero the master mute bus; emits audio:muteChanged on change
  unmute(): void;                                   // restore master gain to masterVolume; emits on change
  setMuted(muted: boolean): void;                   // explicit; emits only on change
  isMuted(): boolean;
  setVolume(channel: Channel, value: number): void; // clamp 0..1; write gain; emit audio:volumeChanged on change
  getVolume(channel: Channel): number;
};
```

### Config

```ts
type Config = {
  masterVolume: number;  // 0..1, the mute-bus gain when unmuted.  @default 1
  sfxVolume: number;     // 0..1.  @default 1
  musicVolume: number;   // 0..1.  @default 1
  muted: boolean;        // start muted (e.g. rehydrated from a prior session).  @default false
  manifest: Readonly<Record<string, string>>;  // name → url, decoded lazily on first load/use.  @default {}
};
```

Volumes are clamped `0..1` on apply. The volume/mute fields double as **rehydration inputs**: a
future `storage`/`platform` plugin passes persisted session values via `pluginConfigs.audio.*`, so
`audio` needs no dependency on storage.

### Gain graph & the mute bus

```
one-shot / music source → channelGain(sfx | music) → masterGain (mute bus) → destination
```

`masterGain` is the **mute bus** — a single node the `platform` adapter zeroes to duck the whole
mix during ad breaks / focus loss. `mute()` sets it to 0; `unmute()` restores it to the stored
`masterVolume`. `setVolume("master")` while muted updates the stored value but keeps the bus at 0
(the new value takes effect on the next `unmute()`). The live `AudioContext` + gain nodes + active
music source are held per app in a module-level `WeakMap<object, AudioEngine>` keyed on `ctx.global`
(the `loop`-plugin pattern), built in `onStart` and torn down in `onStop`. Plugin **State** holds
only the session-serializable mirror: `{ muted, volumes: { master, sfx, music }, buffers: Map<string,
AudioBufferLike>, unlocked }`.

### No autoplay — `unlock()` gates playback

Browsers start an `AudioContext` suspended and block audio until a user gesture. `state.unlocked`
starts `false`, and `play()` / `playMusic()` no-op (debug log) until `unlock()` (idempotent) resumes
the context from within a real gesture. `load` decodes via `fetch` + `AudioContext.decodeAudioData`
and caches per name — a second `load` of a cached name is a no-op (one decode per name).

### Lifecycle & headless

`onStart` builds the `AudioContext` + gain graph from config and stores the engine in the WeakMap;
`onStop` stops the active music source, calls `AudioContext.close()`, and deletes the entry
(idempotent). The `AudioContext` is a real long-lived resource — exactly the `onStart`/`onStop` case.
**Headless-safe:** when no `AudioContext` constructor exists (SSR / tests), `onStart` records a
headless engine and every method **no-ops without throwing** (playback methods log a debug line;
`isMuted`/`getVolume` still return the state mirror). The WebAudio surface is read structurally off
`globalThis` (`AudioContext` / `webkitAudioContext` / `fetch`), all optional — the DOM `lib` is
intentionally absent from tsconfig, so the WebAudio types are declared as minimal structural
interfaces (`AudioContextLike`, `GainNodeLike`, …) in `engine.ts`, keeping the shipped `.d.ts`
free of ambient DOM dependencies.

### Events

`audio:muteChanged { muted }` and `audio:volumeChanged { channel, value }` — coarse, non-hot-path
milestones emitted only on an actual change (see §3). `play`/`playMusic`/`stopMusic` emit nothing.

### Example

```ts
const app = createApp({
  pluginConfigs: { audio: { musicVolume: 0.6, manifest: { jump: "sfx/jump.webm", theme: "music/theme.webm" } } }
});
await app.start();

await app.audio.unlock();          // on the first user gesture (no autoplay before this)
await app.audio.load("jump");      // url resolved from the manifest
app.audio.play("jump", { volume: 0.8 });
app.audio.playMusic("theme", { fadeIn: 0.5 });

// A platform adapter, on an ad break — one call ducks the whole mix:
app.audio.mute();
// …ad plays…
app.audio.unmute();
```

Runtime packages: **none** (WebAudio globals only; no `howler`, no `pixi.js`).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 14. storage — versioned key/value save persistence (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Namespaced, versioned key/value save persistence with a migration chain behind a pluggable
synchronous `StorageBackend` seam. **Safe by construction**: every read/write degrades to an
in-memory `Map` instead of throwing when `localStorage` is partitioned, blocked, quota-full, or
absent (iframe embeds, private mode, SSR/tests). Values are JSON-serialized under a
`${namespace}:${key}` prefix. Foundational (Wave 1) — no game-plugin deps, no new package deps, no
events, no hooks, no lifecycle.

### Public API (src/plugins/storage/types.ts) — `app.storage`

```ts
get<T>(key: string, fallback?: T): T | undefined  // JSON-parse; fallback on missing/unparseable/unavailable; triggers lazy migration; never throws
set(key: string, value: unknown): boolean         // JSON-serialize + write; false on a rejected write / unserializable value; never throws
has(key: string): boolean
remove(key: string): void                          // no-op if absent
clear(): void                                      // wipe every namespace key, then re-stamp the schema version
keys(): string[]                                   // namespace keys, `${namespace}:` prefix stripped, meta key excluded; [] on failure
isPersistent(): boolean                            // false on the in-memory fallback / an injected non-persistent backend
getVersion(): number                               // schema version in effect after any lazy migration
setBackend(backend: StorageBackend): void          // inject a custom backend (platform's CrazyGames adapter); resets `migrated`
```

### Exported types

```ts
type Snapshot = Record<string, unknown>;                     // un-prefixed key → parsed value, the whole-namespace bag a migration transforms
type Migration = (snapshot: Snapshot) => Snapshot;           // migrations[n] upgrades a v(n-1) snapshot to vn (pure)
type StorageBackend = {                                      // the synchronous persistence seam — implementations MUST NOT throw
  getItem(key: string): string | null;                      // mirrors the Web Storage API so a localStorage-shaped backend is drop-in
  setItem(key: string, value: string): boolean;             // false on a failed write (quota / blocked)
  removeItem(key: string): void;
  keys(prefix: string): string[];                           // full keys beginning with `${namespace}:`
  readonly persistent: boolean;                             // false = in-memory only (does not survive a reload)
};
```

### Config

`namespace` (`@default "game"`) · `version` (`@default 1`) · `migrations` (`Record<number, Migration>`,
`@default {}`). All optional at the `pluginConfigs.storage` boundary (shallow-merged over the defaults).

### Versioned schema & lazy migration

The migration chain runs **lazily on first access**, once (memoized by `state.migrated`) — not at a
lifecycle hook. This is load-bearing: it guarantees the chain runs against whichever backend is
active at first read, including one the `platform` plugin injects via `setBackend()` *after*
storage starts (an eager `onStart` migration would migrate the wrong, default backend). The stored
version lives under a reserved meta key. A fresh store is stamped with no migration; a store already
at `version` is untouched; `migrations[storedVersion+1 .. version]` are folded over the snapshot in
order on an upgrade; a store **newer** than the app is left intact with a `ctx.log.warn` (no
downgrade). `setBackend()` resets `migrated` so an injected backend re-migrates on its next access.

### Safe default backend (IoC seam)

The default backend probes `globalThis.localStorage` behind a guard (structural `WebStorageLike` — no
DOM `lib`, mirroring `audio`'s structural WebAudio types). A usable store is wrapped (every method
guarded — a quota `setItem` returns `false`, a blocked read returns `null`); otherwise an in-memory
