`Map` (`persistent: false`) is used. `storage` owns the `StorageBackend` interface and ships this safe
default; the `platform` plugin (`depends: [storagePlugin]`) implements it over the CrazyGames
data API and calls `app.storage.setBackend(...)` at its own `onStart` — storage never imports
`platform`, keeping it at the bottom of the dependency graph. The API additionally guards every
backend call, so no method throws even against a misbehaving injected backend.

### Events

None. Persisting another plugin's state (e.g. `audio`'s mute/volume) is the caller's job — whoever
hooks `audio:muteChanged` / `audio:volumeChanged` then calls `app.storage.set(...)`.

### Example

```ts
const app = createApp({
  pluginConfigs: {
    storage: {
      namespace: "save",
      version: 2,
      migrations: { 2: (snapshot) => ({ ...snapshot, coins: (snapshot.coins as number) ?? 0 }) }
    }
  }
});
await app.start();

app.storage.set("bestHeight", 128);
const best = app.storage.get<number>("bestHeight", 0); // 128, or 0 on a fresh/blocked store
if (!app.storage.isPersistent()) warnProgressNotSaved();

// The platform adapter, at its own onStart, routes saves through CrazyGames:
app.storage.setBackend(crazyGamesBackend);             // re-migrates on next read
```

Runtime packages: **none** (`localStorage` + `JSON` are browser/language globals; no `idb`/`localforage`).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 15. platform — portal-SDK adapter layer (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `platform` plugin lets one game codebase ship as a monetizable build for **CrazyGames, Poki, and
Newgrounds** (plus a **no-op / standalone** adapter for local dev). One internal `PortalAdapter`
interface, one implementation per portal in `adapters/`, and the **active adapter is selected per build
via `ctx.env`** — so the same game code produces a distinct per-portal bundle. Depends on `audio`,
`loop`, and `storage` (their APIs via `ctx.require`); **no new package dependency** (portal SDKs are
runtime-injected from the portal CDN and typed structurally, so the shipped `.d.ts` is SDK-ambient-free).

Config (`pluginConfigs.platform`):

```ts
export type Portal = "crazygames" | "poki" | "newgrounds" | "none";
export type Config = {
  portal: Portal | "auto";        // @default "auto" — "auto" resolves the portal from ctx.env
  portalEnvVar: string;           // @default "GAME_PORTAL" — env var read (case-insensitive) when "auto"
  pauseOnAd: boolean;             // @default true — pause loop + mute audio for every ad (and on focus loss)
  minInterstitialSeconds: number; // @default 60 — frequency cap between commercialBreak() shows
  useNativeStorage: boolean;      // @default true — inject the adapter's native StorageBackend when it has one
  persistAudioPrefs: boolean;     // @default true — persist + rehydrate audio mute/volume through storage
};
```

Public API (`app.platform`):

```ts
getPortal(): Portal                 // the portal resolved at onStart ("none" for local dev / unknown env)
gameplayStart(): void               // signal active gameplay started (portals gate ad timing/analytics on it)
gameplayStop(): void                // signal gameplay stopped (menu / pause / game-over)
loadingStart(): void                // signal loading started (usually driven by onStart)
loadingFinished(): void             // signal loading finished (game interactive; driven by onStart)
commercialBreak(): Promise<void>    // interstitial; pauses loop + mutes audio, restores on settle; frequency-capped; never rejects
rewardedAd(): Promise<boolean>      // rewarded; same coordination; true = watched-to-completion; noop adapter → true
isAdPlaying(): boolean
```

**Env-selected adapter.** `config.portal` defaults to `"auto"`, resolving the active portal from
`ctx.env.get(config.portalEnvVar)` (default `GAME_PORTAL`, matched case-insensitively) at `onStart`;
an explicit `config.portal` overrides the env lookup. Unknown/absent env — or a headless runtime with
no `window` — resolves to the inert **`none`** adapter. `selectAdapter(portal)` maps each `Portal` to
its factory; unknown → `noop`.

**Ads are capture-then-restore.** `commercialBreak()` / `rewardedAd()` capture `loop.isRunning()` +
`audio.isMuted()` *before* the ad, pause (`loop.stop()`) + mute (`audio.mute()`), and on settle
(resolve **or** reject) restore only what they changed — `loop.start()` only if it was running,
`audio.unmute()` only if audio was not already muted. A re-entrancy guard (`state.adPlaying`) makes a
second ad call while one is in flight a no-op (`commercialBreak` resolves; `rewardedAd` → `false`);
`commercialBreak` honours the `minInterstitialSeconds` cap (a call inside the window resolves as a
no-show). Neither ever rejects to the caller. The same coordination drives the game's own `window`
focus/blur + visibilitychange listeners (pause + mute on focus loss, restore on regain). Iframe-safe —
only the game's own `window` is read, never `window.top`.

**PortalAdapter seam** (in `types.ts`): `{ portal; init(ctx): Promise<void>; gameplayStart/Stop;
loadingStart/Finished; commercialBreak(): Promise<void>; rewardedAd(): Promise<boolean>; storageBackend?;
destroy() }`. Each concrete adapter loads its portal SDK (`window.CrazyGames` / `window.PokiSDK` /
`window.Newgrounds` — expected on the page, best-effort `<script>`-injected otherwise) and types it
**structurally** (no portal npm package). A degraded (SDK-unavailable) adapter no-ops its ads.

**CrazyGames async→sync storage bridge.** CrazyGames provides a portal-native `StorageBackend` over its
async data API. Since `storage`'s `StorageBackend` is synchronous + non-throwing, the adapter's `init()`
awaits a one-time hydrate of an in-memory write-through snapshot: `getItem`/`keys` read the snapshot,
`setItem`/`removeItem` mutate it synchronously (returning `true`) and flush to the portal best-effort.
It is injected via `storage.setBackend()` only when `useNativeStorage` and the adapter provides one —
Poki/Newgrounds provide none, keeping `storage`'s localStorage default. Because `storage` migrates
lazily and `setBackend()` resets its `migrated` flag, migration correctly targets the hydrated snapshot.

**Audio-pref persistence** (when `persistAudioPrefs`, default): hooks `audio:muteChanged` →
`storage.set("audio.muted", …)` and `audio:volumeChanged` → `storage.set("audio.volume." + channel, …)`,
and at `onStart` (after any native-backend injection) rehydrates those keys back into `audio` via
`setMuted` / `setVolume`. A fresh store leaves `audio` at its configured defaults. This closes the
persistence the `audio` and `storage` specs deferred to "a future storage/platform plugin".

**Lifecycle.** `onStart`/`onStop` are justified — the loaded SDK + focus/visibility DOM listeners are a
real resource, held per app in a `ctx.global` WeakMap (`platformRegistry`, the `audio`/`loop` pattern);
`State` holds only the serializable session mirror (resolved portal, ad-flight flag, last-interstitial
timestamp). `onStart` resolves the portal, builds + `init()`s the adapter, injects the backend,
rehydrates prefs, registers listeners, records the runtime, and emits `platform:ready`. `onStop` removes
the listeners, calls `adapter.destroy()`, and clears the runtime.

Events: `platform:ready` `{ portal }`, `platform:adStart` `{ type }`, `platform:adEnd`
`{ type, rewarded? }` (declared via `register.map<Events>`). Depends: `audio` (mute/unmute + rehydrate),
`loop` (pause/resume), `storage` (backend injection + pref persistence). Runtime packages: **none**.

```ts
// Build each portal's bundle from the same game code (GAME_PORTAL selects the adapter):
//   GAME_PORTAL=crazygames bun run build
const rewarded = await app.platform.rewardedAd(); // loop paused + audio muted during, restored after
if (rewarded) grantExtraLife();

app.platform.gameplayStop();
await app.platform.commercialBreak();             // interstitial, frequency-capped, never rejects
app.platform.gameplayStart();
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 16. vfx — game-juice / visual-feedback layer (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `vfx` plugin adds the **juice** that makes hits, pickups, and score changes *feel* good:
**ECS-native particle emitters** + one-shot bursts, **trauma-based screen shake**, **Transform
scale-pop**, **floating damage/score text**, and a set of **pure easing curves + `lerp`**. It is a
pure **API your own game code calls** (`app.vfx.burst()` / `shake()` / `pop()` / `floatText()` from
collision and scoring systems), so vfx never couples to any specific gameplay event. Every effect
runs as a scheduler system over ordinary ECS entities, so every particle and emitter is queryable
through `world.query(...)` and MCP-introspectable by name. Depends on `ecs`, `scheduler`, and
`renderer` (their APIs via `ctx.require`); **no new package dependency** — Pixi `Graphics`/`Text`
arrive via `renderer`, and Pixi imports are confined to vfx's own domain files. Emits **no** kernel
events (per-frame hot path). **Headless-safe.**

Config (`pluginConfigs.vfx`):

```ts
export type Config = {
  maxParticles: number;   // @default 1000 — global cap on simultaneously-live particles across every emitter + burst
  shakeDecay: number;     // @default 1.8 — screen-shake trauma decay in units/second (trauma is 0..1)
  shakeMaxOffset: number; // @default 24 — max stage offset in px at trauma = 1 (offset scales with trauma²)
  defaultColor: number;   // @default 0xffffff — fallback particle/text color when a spec omits `color`
};
```

Public API (`app.vfx`):

```ts
createEmitter(spec: EmitterSpec): Entity                       // persistent emitter entity (Emitter + Transform at spec.x/y); required: rate, speed, lifetime
configureEmitter(emitter: Entity, patch: Partial<EmitterSpec>): void  // shallow-merge emission params (position moves via the Transform, not here)
setEmitterEnabled(emitter: Entity, enabled: boolean): void     // pause/resume emission; existing particles stay alive
removeEmitter(emitter: Entity): void                           // despawn the emitter AND only its own live particles (a per-particle owner back-ref scopes the sweep)
burst(x: number, y: number, spec: BurstSpec): void             // one-shot spark/explosion/pickup; required: count, speed, lifetime; defaults to full-circle spread
shake(amplitude: number, duration: number): void               // bank screen-shake trauma (0..1); the render-stage system offsets the stage root by trauma² · shakeMaxOffset
stopShake(): void                                              // clear trauma + reset the stage offset to (0,0) immediately
pop(entity: Entity, opts?: PopOptions): void                   // scale-pop a Transform to opts.scale× (@default 1.3) over opts.duration (@default 0.15s) and back; no-op without a Transform
flash(entity: Entity, opts?: FlashOptions): void               // hit-flash: snap view.tint to opts.color (@default white) then ease back to the captured base over opts.duration (@default 0.12s); alive entity; tint only with a view (headless → ages out); re-call refreshes
floatText(x: number, y: number, text: string, opts?: FloatTextOptions): Entity  // rising, alpha-fading floating number/text (e.g. "+50"); returns its entity handle
readonly easing: Readonly<Record<EasingName, (t: number) => number>>  // frozen curve table (see below)
lerp(a: number, b: number, t: number): number                  // linear interpolation a + (b − a) * t
// EasingName = "linear" | "easeInQuad" | "easeOutQuad" | "easeInOutQuad" | "easeOutCubic" | "easeOutBack" | "easeOutElastic".
// EmitterSpec: required rate/speed/lifetime; optional x/y/angle/spread/speedVariance/lifetimeVariance/startScale/endScale/radius/color/gravityX/gravityY/enabled (config/sane defaults).
// BurstSpec: required count/speed/lifetime; same optional emission fields (spread @default π → full circle); no x/y (passed as burst args).
// FloatTextOptions: lifetime/riseSpeed/startAlpha/endAlpha/color/fontSize. PopOptions: scale (@default 1.3) / duration (@default 0.15s).
// Every effectful method is a guarded no-op before app.start() (returning a dead handle where it must return one) and on dead / wrong-type entities.
```

### ECS-native particles (no `@pixi/particle-emitter`)

An emitter is an **entity** carrying a named `Emitter` component; a particle is an **entity** carrying
`renderer.Transform` + a named `Particle`. The emit system spawns particles inside the `"update"`
stage; because the ECS command buffer flushes before the `"sync"` stage, an emitted particle is
**visible the same frame**. Particles are drawn via `renderer.attachPrimitive` (a Pixi `Graphics`), so
the **renderer owns their entire view lifecycle** — stage-add, per-tick sync from `Transform`, and
disposal on despawn — and vfx keeps no per-particle handle. Particles **fade by scaling out**
(`startScale → endScale ≈ 0`) rather than by alpha, so they render straight through the renderer's
primitive path with zero new dependency. A `ParticleContainer`-backed batch path for *thousands* of
particles is a documented deferred optimization; the target here is "hundreds at 60 fps". A third-party
emitter was rejected because it adds a runtime dependency and a second particle lifecycle *outside* the
world (invisible to `world.query`, MCP introspection, and scene-unload reconciliation).

### Effects

- **Emitters** — persistent particle sources. `createEmitter` spawns an emitter entity; reconfigure it
  live with `configureEmitter`, pause/resume with `setEmitterEnabled`, tear it down (with only its own
  live particles) via `removeEmitter`. Emission honours a fractional accumulator, so `rate = 60` yields
  ≈1 particle per 1/60 s tick with no double-spawn.
- **Burst** — one-shot `burst(x, y, spec)` for hit sparks, explosions, pickups; no persistent entity is
  retained; particles default to a full-circle spread.
- **Screen shake** — trauma-based. `shake(amplitude, duration)` banks trauma (0..1); the render-stage
  system offsets the Pixi stage root by `trauma² · shakeMaxOffset` in a random direction and decays
  trauma by `shakeDecay` each second, snapping back to (0, 0) at rest. `stopShake()` clears it
  immediately.
- **Pop** — `pop(entity, { scale, duration })` gives any entity's Transform a squash-and-return scale
  pulse; it captures and restores the entity's **exact** base scale, and re-calling refreshes the pop
  without recapturing a mid-pop scale.
- **Hit-flash** — `flash(entity, { color, duration })` tints the entity's view to `color` (default
  white) then eases it back to the captured base tint over `duration` (default 0.12 s), restoring it
  **exactly**; re-calling refreshes without recapturing a mid-flash tint. It reads the view via the
  renderer's `getEntityView` and writes `view.tint` directly (tint is not a Transform channel);
  headless / view-less entities age the effect out with no tint write.
- **Floating text** — `floatText(x, y, text, opts)` spawns a rising, alpha-fading `Text` (e.g. `"+50"`
  damage numbers). vfx retains this one handle to fade its alpha each frame (the renderer's sync system
  positions it but never touches alpha); it despawns itself at end of life.
- **Easing** — `app.vfx.easing` is a frozen table of pure `f(t): [0,1]→[0,1]` curves (`linear`,
  `easeInQuad`, `easeOutQuad`, `easeInOutQuad`, `easeOutCubic`, `easeOutBack`, `easeOutElastic`) plus
  `app.vfx.lerp`. Reused internally and exported for consumer juice (and a future `tween` plugin — vfx
  ships the easing curves it needs, **not** a general property-tween scheduler).

### Global particle cap

A single `maxParticles` budget spans every emitter and burst. At the cap, further emission is
**dropped** (`ctx.log` debug once per over-budget frame) — emitters never back up an unbounded queue.
The `particleCount` state is maintained by the emit/particle systems.

### Lifecycle & headless

`onStart` is **deps-ready wiring** (the renderer's own onStart shape — not a per-frame or
resource-owning path): after `ecs`/`scheduler`/`renderer` have started it (1) captures
`renderer.Transform` (reading it earlier throws), (2) defines the five named components
(`Emitter`/`Particle`/`Pop`/`Flash`/`FloatingText`) so they exist before any spawn and are MCP-introspectable
by name, and (3) registers the six effect systems (emit/particle/pop/flash/floating in `"update"`, shake in
`"render"`). There is **no `onStop`**: vfx owns no external OS/GPU resource of its own — every view it
builds is registered with the renderer (particle `Graphics` via `attachPrimitive`, floating-text `Text`
via `attach`), so the renderer disposes them; vfx's own state (the `Text` handle map, `trauma`,
`particleCount`) is plain GC-able data (there is **no** `ctx.global` WeakMap). **Headless-safe:** every
system is registered and runs identically headless — entities simulate with no visual; only Pixi *view
creation* guards on `renderer.getStage()` being defined (headless particles never build a `Graphics`,
headless floating text never builds a `Text`, and the shake system decays trauma without writing the
absent stage). Nothing throws.

Events: **none.** Particle emission, shake, pop, and floating text are per-frame hot-path work with no
discrete milestone worth a kernel event — the same stance as `ecs`, `renderer`, `scheduler`, and `loop`
(see §3). Compose vfx with other plugins at the app layer (e.g. `vfx.burst()` + `audio.play()` on the
same hit). Depends: `ecs` (the five named components, `spawn`/`despawn`, `query(...).updateEach`,
`add`/`remove`/`get`/`set`/`has`/`isAlive`), `scheduler` (`addSystem` for the six systems; `dt` via the
`(world, dt)` signature), `renderer` (`Transform`, `attachPrimitive`, `attach` + `getStage`,
`getEntityView`, `markDirty`) — **not** `loop` (dt flows through the system signature) and **not** `audio` (a title
composes them at the app layer). Runtime packages: **none** (Pixi via `renderer`).

```ts
// In a collision system, on an enemy hit:
app.vfx.burst(hit.x, hit.y, { count: 16, speed: 220, lifetime: 0.5, color: 0xffcc00, radius: 3 });
app.vfx.pop(enemy, { scale: 1.4, duration: 0.12 });   // squash-pop the sprite
app.vfx.flash(enemy, { color: 0xffffff, duration: 0.12 }); // white hit-flash the sprite
app.vfx.floatText(hit.x, hit.y - 20, "+50", { color: 0xffffff });
app.vfx.shake(0.5, 0.3);                               // punchy screen shake

// A persistent trail emitter following the player, moved each frame via its Transform:
const trail = app.vfx.createEmitter({ rate: 80, speed: 40, spread: 0.3, lifetime: 0.6, color: 0x66ccff });
app.ecs.set(trail, app.renderer.Transform, { x: player.x, y: player.y });
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 17. ui — PixiJS-native game-UI layer (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **game-UI layer**: a **screen stack** (title / pause / game-over / upgrade-choice modal cards), a
small **declarative widget set** (`label` / `button` / `panel` / `bar`), a **persistent HUD**, and
**pointer/touch hit-testing** — all rendered **natively into the renderer's Pixi stage**. Widgets are
described as **plain data** (no Pixi types leak into the specs); `ui` builds the Pixi objects once and
returns **opaque handles** for later mutation (retained-mode, not a per-frame rebuild). Depends on
`renderer` (`getStage()` to parent the UI root), `scheduler` (`addSystem("update", …)`), and `input`
(`snapshot().pointer`). Emits **no** events. **Headless-safe**. **No new package dependency** (Pixi via
`renderer`).

**PixiJS-native, not DOM islands via `@moku-labs/web`.** A single-canvas UI adds zero new dependency,
keeps the whole game in one portable surface (the iframe/portal target), and shares one coordinate space
with the renderer's shake/camera — matching the `audio` (native WebAudio) and `vfx` (ECS-native
particles) precedent. A responsive layout engine (safe-area insets, reflow, flex/grid) is a documented
deferred enhancement; this plugin ships absolute screen-space positioning with anchors.

**Screen stack (modal capture).** `pushScreen(spec)` builds one Pixi `Container` — an optional
full-viewport dimming backdrop behind a set of widgets — and adds it on top of the stack. Only the TOP
screen's buttons are tappable while the stack is non-empty (a modal captures pointer input away from the
HUD and lower screens). `popScreen`/`replaceScreen`/`clearScreens` destroy the container(s) they remove;
the HUD is never touched. `topScreen()` / `screenCount()` read the stack.

**Widgets (plain data).** `label` (a Pixi `Text`), `button` (bg + centered caption, with an explicit
`width`/`height` hit-area or a documented text-estimate fallback), `panel` (a filled/rounded card whose
`children` are positioned relative to its origin — nested hit-rects accumulate the panel offset), and
`bar` (a track + a fill scaled horizontally to `value/max`, so `setValue` is a cheap `scale.x` write).
`WidgetSpec` is a discriminated union on `kind`; `ScreenSpec = { backdrop?, widgets }`. Widgets given a
spec `id` are addressable inside a pushed screen via `getWidget(screen, id)` (recursive through panels).

**HUD + live mutation.** `addHud(spec)` builds a persistent widget outside the stack (score `label`,
health `bar`, pause `button`); HUD buttons are tappable only when the stack is empty (a modal captures
them). Mutate any live widget through its handle: `setText` (label/button caption), `setValue` (bar,
clamped to `[0, max]`), `setVisible`, and `removeHud`.

**Pointer hit-testing.** One `"update"`-stage system reads `input.snapshot().pointer` each frame and
computes its own press/release edges from the button bitmask (the input snapshot exposes
`justPressed`/`justReleased` for keys only, not pointer buttons). A button's `onTap` fires on pointer-up
over the SAME button armed on pointer-down — a press that drifts off, or a release over an un-armed
button, fires nothing. Touch is first-class for free: `input.pointer` is fed by DOM pointer events,
which unify mouse + touch.

**Lifecycle & disposal.** `onStart` (deps-ready wiring, the renderer/`vfx` shape) captures
`renderer.getStage()`, builds the UI root over the game entities when a stage exists, and registers the
hit-test system. There is NO `onStop`: every Pixi object is parented under the renderer-owned stage, so
the renderer disposes the whole subtree; in-run disposal (`popScreen`/`clearScreens`/`replaceScreen`/
`removeHud`) is the API's job. Headless-safe: with `renderer.getStage()` undefined, the root is never
built and every API method + the system are guarded no-ops.

Public API (`app.ui`):

```ts
pushScreen(spec: ScreenSpec): ScreenHandle       // build + push a screen (widgets + optional backdrop)
popScreen(): void                                // remove + destroy the top screen
replaceScreen(spec: ScreenSpec): ScreenHandle    // popScreen() then pushScreen(spec)
clearScreens(): void                             // remove + destroy every screen (HUD untouched)
topScreen(): ScreenHandle | undefined            // the top screen's handle, or undefined when empty
screenCount(): number                            // stack depth
addHud(spec: WidgetSpec): WidgetHandle           // persistent HUD widget outside the stack
removeHud(handle: WidgetHandle): void            // remove + destroy a HUD widget
getWidget(screen: ScreenHandle, id: string): WidgetHandle | undefined  // resolve a screen widget by spec id
setText(handle: WidgetHandle, text: string): void
setValue(handle: WidgetHandle, value: number): void
setVisible(handle: WidgetHandle, visible: boolean): void
getRoot(): Container | undefined                 // the UI root Container (advanced), or undefined before start / headless
```

Config (theme defaults + reference viewport): `textColor` (0xffffff), `fontSize` (20), `fontFamily`
("sans-serif"), `buttonColor` (0x3355ff), `buttonHoverColor` (0x4466ff), `panelColor` (0x141821),
`panelAlpha` (0.92), `backdropColor` (0x000000), `backdropAlpha` (0.6), `padding` (12), `width` (800),
`height` (600). `width`/`height` are the reference viewport a modal backdrop fills — the renderer
exposes no canvas dimensions via its API (only `getStage()`), so set them to match the renderer's canvas.

```ts
// Title screen with a Play button; a HUD score + health bar during gameplay:
app.ui.pushScreen({ widgets: [
  { kind: "label", text: "ASCEND", x: 400, y: 200, fontSize: 48 },
  { kind: "button", text: "Play", x: 400, y: 320, width: 200, height: 56,
    onTap: () => { app.ui.popScreen(); app.loop.start(); } }
]});

const score = app.ui.addHud({ kind: "label", text: "0", x: 16, y: 16, anchor: { x: 0, y: 0 } });
const hp = app.ui.addHud({ kind: "bar", value: 100, max: 100, x: 16, y: 44, width: 160, height: 12 });
app.scheduler.addSystem("update", () => {
  app.ui.setText(score, String(currentScore));
  app.ui.setValue(hp, player.health);
});
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 18. tween — shared ECS-agnostic tweening (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The reusable animation primitive `vfx`, `ui`, and a future `camera` can all share. Unlike vfx's
ECS-native particles, a tween is agnostic to *what* it animates: it reads and writes the numeric fields
of any plain mutable object you hand it — a camera `{ x, y }`, a UI element's position, an audio-gain
holder, a score counter. Advanced by one scheduler `"update"`-stage system on the game clock, so tweens
**pause exactly when the loop pauses** (e.g. during a portal ad break) with zero coupling to `platform`
or `loop` (no dependency edge, no hook). Emits no events. Headless-safe. Depends on `scheduler` only.

**Design calls:** (1) **Imperative tweens over plain objects**, not an ECS `Tween` component — the only
form that serves vfx (entities), ui (Pixi handles), and camera (`{x,y,zoom}`) alike; a consumer who wants
to tween an ECS value reads it, tweens a scratch object, and writes it back in `onUpdate` (one-line escape
hatch). (2) **Advanced by a scheduler system** (not a private rAF/timer) — so a paused loop freezes tweens
for free. (3) **Owns its own `easing.ts` with the vfx-identical 7 curves** — the `add` flow must not touch
vfx; identical curves make a later `vfx → tween` dedupe a drop-in. (4) **onStart-only** (`@no-resource-check`
deps-ready wiring: register the advance system + flip `started`); **NO onStop** — tweens are plain GC-able
data, the system owns no external resource (the vfx precedent). (5) Before-start / over-`maxActive` creators
warn + return a **dead handle** (no hot-path throw). (6) `done` settles on complete OR stop (never hangs).
(7) `NumericProperties<T>` restricts `to`/`from` to numeric keys at compile time.

Public API (`app.tween`):

```ts
to<T>(target: T, props: NumericProperties<T>, opts?: TweenOptions): TweenHandle;   // capture start = current, tween to props values (mutates target); start captured at creation (delay does not defer it); non-finite props skipped (debug-logged)
from<T>(target: T, props: NumericProperties<T>, opts?: TweenOptions): TweenHandle; // apply the "from" values immediately, then tween back to the current values
value(from: number, to: number, opts: ValueTweenOptions): TweenHandle;            // tween a bare scalar, driving opts.onUpdate(value) each frame (onUpdate REQUIRED — there is no target)
killAll(): void;                                 // stop + drop every active tween (no onComplete; each done settles)
count(): number;                                 // number of active tweens
readonly easing: Readonly<Record<EasingName, (t: number) => number>>;  // the frozen 7-curve table (parity with vfx)
lerp(a: number, b: number, t: number): number;   // linear interpolation a + (b − a) * t (unclamped)

// TweenOptions: { duration?, easing?, delay?, repeat?, yoyo?, onUpdate?(progress), onComplete?() }
//   duration @default config.defaultDuration · easing (EasingName | (t)=>number) @default config.defaultEasing ·
//   delay @default 0 (does NOT defer start capture) · repeat @default 0 (Infinity allowed) · yoyo @default false (ping-pong)
// ValueTweenOptions = TweenOptions & { onUpdate(value): void }  (onUpdate required)
// TweenHandle: stop() (cancel now, no onComplete, settles done, idempotent) · pause()/resume() · readonly active · readonly done: Promise<void> (settles on complete OR stop — never rejects/hangs)
// EasingName: "linear" | "easeInQuad" | "easeOutQuad" | "easeInOutQuad" | "easeOutCubic" | "easeOutBack" | "easeOutElastic"
```

```ts
// Smooth camera pan (any plain { x, y } holder):
app.tween.to(camera, { x: 640, y: 360 }, { duration: 0.6, easing: "easeOutCubic" });
// Slide a UI card in from off-screen, then fire a callback:
app.tween.from(card, { y: -200 }, { duration: 0.4, easing: "easeOutBack", onComplete: enableInput });
// Ramp master volume during a fade-out (no target object — drive a scalar):
await app.tween.value(1, 0, { duration: 0.5, onUpdate: v => app.audio.setVolume("master", v) }).done;
```

**Config** (`pluginConfigs.tween`): `defaultDuration` (@default 0.3), `defaultEasing` (@default
`"easeOutCubic"`), `updateStage` (@default `"update"`), `maxActive` (@default 2048).

**Advance semantics:** the `"update"`-stage system steps each non-paused record by `dt` — burns down
`delay` (carrying overflow into elapsed), applies `easingFn(clamp(elapsed/duration))` (yoyo reverses the
curve on odd iterations), and at the iteration boundary repeats (carry overflow, `repeat--`) or completes
(fire `onComplete`, settle `done`, drop the record). Completed ids are deleted after a snapshot pass, so a
re-entrant `onComplete` that creates a new tween does not advance it the same frame. `duration: 0` is
coerced to a tiny epsilon so it resolves to the end on the first tick.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 19. camera — 2D follow/shake/parallax camera (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The missing "where the viewport looks" layer, and the first real **consumer of `app.tween`**. A 2D game
camera that `follow`s a moving target with per-frame exponential smoothing, pans/zooms/rotates instantly
or animated over time (the animated forms delegate to `app.tween`, so they are pause-safe for free),
applies a decaying **screen shake** (also `app.tween`-driven), renders **parallax** across world-space
**layer containers it owns**, and maps points between screen and world space. Depends on `renderer` +
`scheduler` + `tween`. Emits no events. Headless-safe and pause-safe. No new package dependency.

**Design calls:** (1) **Transforms world-space LAYER containers it OWNS, NOT the renderer root stage** —
a default `world` `Container` parented at stage index 0 (`stage.addChildAt(world, 0)`, below the ui
overlay) plus optional parallax layers via `addLayer(name, factor)`. Transforming the root stage would
drag the HUD along with the world and makes parallax impossible (one stage = one plane); the world-vs-screen
split is the idiomatic Pixi camera. Composes with the renderer's `"sync"` system: the parent (a camera
layer) carries the *camera* transform, the child view carries its *entity* `Transform` — they multiply
correctly. *Limitation:* `renderer.attachPrimitive` self-parents to the stage root → NOT camera-transformed;
the camera path is `renderer.attach` + `camera.world`/a layer. (2) **The follow target is a structural
`{ x, y }` read each frame → NO `ecs` dependency** — a Pixi `Container`, an entity `Transform` value, and a
bare point all satisfy it; smoothing reuses `app.tween.lerp` (the single canonical `lerp`). `follow()` with
no arg clears (uses `undefined` throughout — no `unicorn/no-null` override). (3) **`moveTo`/`zoomTo`/
`rotateTo`/`shake` consume `app.tween`** (the reason for the tween edge) → smooth, pause-safe camera work
for free; instant setters bypass tween; `moveTo` clears follow; `shake` replaces any in-flight shake.
(4) **Apply system in `"sync"`** (after physics writes the target, before render); **headless-safe** — no
stage → layers `undefined`, container writes are guarded no-ops, but numeric state (centre/zoom/rotation)
and `screenToWorld`/`worldToScreen` still track. (5) **onStart-only** (`@no-resource-check` deps-ready
wiring in `lifecycle.ts`: capture renderer/scheduler/tween, seed zoom, build the world `Container`, register
the apply system, flip `started`); **NO onStop** — every `Container` is parented under the renderer-owned
stage (the renderer disposes the subtree), the system owns no external resource, and `state.tween` is a
captured reference.

Public API (`app.camera`):

```ts
readonly world: Container | undefined;             // the default world layer (factor 1); undefined headless
addLayer(name: string, factor: number): Container | undefined; // create/return a named parallax layer (idempotent by name; factor NOT overwritten); undefined headless
layer(name: string): Container | undefined;        // look up a previously-added layer container
follow(target?: { readonly x: number; readonly y: number }): void; // ease the centre toward a LIVE { x, y } each frame; no arg clears
setPosition(x: number, y: number): void;           // snap the centre immediately (clears follow)
moveTo(x: number, y: number, opts?: MoveOptions): TweenHandle;  // animated pan via app.tween (clears follow)
getPosition(): Point;                              // the current centre (a fresh copy — never the mutable state object)
setZoom(zoom: number): void;                       // set zoom immediately, clamped to [minZoom, maxZoom]
zoomTo(zoom: number, opts?: MoveOptions): TweenHandle;          // animated zoom via app.tween (final value clamped)
getZoom(): number;
setRotation(radians: number): void;                // set rotation immediately
rotateTo(radians: number, opts?: MoveOptions): TweenHandle;     // animated rotate via app.tween
getRotation(): number;
shake(intensity: number, duration: number, opts?: ShakeOptions): void; // additive screen shake decaying to 0 over duration s via app.tween; replaces any in-flight shake
screenToWorld(point: Point): Point;                // map a screen point to world space (picking); pure, works before start
worldToScreen(point: Point): Point;                // map a world point to screen space (place UI over a world object); pure

// MoveOptions = Pick<TweenOptions, "duration" | "easing" | "delay" | "onComplete" | "onUpdate">  (NO repeat/yoyo)
// ShakeOptions = { easing?: Easing }  (the intensity → 0 decay curve, @default "linear")
// Point = { x: number; y: number }  ·  FollowTarget = { readonly x: number; readonly y: number }
// Layer = { readonly container: Container; factor: number }  (0 = static … 1 = world … >1 = foreground)
```

```ts
// Put world content under the camera; the HUD stays screen-fixed on the stage root.
app.camera.world?.addChild(playerSprite);
const far = app.camera.addLayer("far", 0.3);       // slow-scrolling background (parallax)
app.camera.follow(playerSprite);                   // follow any live { x, y }
await app.camera.moveTo(640, 360, { duration: 0.6, easing: "easeOutCubic" }).done;
app.camera.shake(16, 0.4);                          // hit impact — decays via app.tween
const screen = app.camera.worldToScreen({ x: enemy.x, y: enemy.y }); // place a marker over a world object
```

**Config** (`pluginConfigs.camera`): `zoom` (@default 1), `minZoom` (@default 0.1), `maxZoom` (@default 10),
`followLerp` (@default 0.15 — per-fixed-step smoothing in `[0,1]`; 1 = snap), `width` (@default 800),
`height` (@default 600), `updateStage` (@default `"sync"`). `width`/`height` are a **reference viewport**
(the renderer exposes no canvas dims via its API — the ui-plugin precedent) used for centering and the
screen↔world math.

**Apply semantics:** the `"sync"`-stage system, each frame, (1) eases the centre toward the follow target
via `app.tween.lerp` (frame-rate-stable under the fixed timestep), (2) computes one random shake offset
vector scaled by the current `shakeIntensity` (injectable `random` for deterministic tests), (3) returns
early when headless (numeric state already updated), else (4) writes each layer `{ container, factor }`:
`scale.set(zoom)`, `rotation`, `pivot.set(center*factor)`, `position.set(width/2 + offX, height/2 + offY)`
— so factor 1 centres `center` at the viewport centre and factor < 1 scrolls slower (parallax). The camera
advances via its **tweens** (stepped by the tween plugin's own `"update"` system), so a paused loop freezes
all camera motion for free.

## 20. Editor subsystem — Layer-2 in-browser editor (#17–#24, additive)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An in-browser, Unity-style editor over the LIVE ECS, added as 8 Layer-2 plugins registered after `mcp`
(`editor-bridge` last). Design pillars: a **single validated write-authority** (`commands`), **poll-on-epoch**
reactivity (no per-frame `emit`), a **two-`createApp` split** (the Layer-3 `@moku-labs/web` editor app is a
separate `createApp` that consumes only `app["editor-bridge"]`), and **pay-for-what-you-use** (a non-editor
game never calls these and `editor-runtime` never gates the scheduler until `enterEdit()`). Per-plugin READMEs
under `src/plugins/<name>/README.md` remain the authoritative API detail; this consolidates the surface.

Extension deltas on already-built plugins (all additive, non-breaking):
- **ecs** — `changeEpoch(): number` (monotone per-write counter, powering poll-on-epoch with NO emit) +
  `setActiveStages(stages | undefined)` / `activeStages()` (the stage gate on `world.tick`; `undefined` = all).
- **scheduler** — forwards `setActiveStages`/`activeStages` to the world (the edit-vs-play mechanism).
- **assets** — `entries(): AssetEntry[]` / `manifest()` / `metadata(alias)` (read-only enumeration for the asset browser).
- **tween** / **vfx** / **camera** — `reset()` (clear transient runtime; `editor-runtime.stop()` calls all three on exit-play).

The 8 editor plugins:
- **commands** (Standard, depends ecs) — the SINGLE validated write-authority; owns the branded, save-durable
  `EditorId`. `apply(command): CommandResult` (returns the inverse), `applyRaw` (primitive editor-history wraps),
  `restore(entities, "reload"|"exit-play")` (non-undoable reseed → emits `commands:restored`),
  `resolve`/`editorIdOf` (validate-before-return recycled-id guard), `setValidator` (inject reflection.validate), `count`.
  `Command` = a discriminated union addressed by component NAME (spawn/despawn/setField/addComponent/removeComponent).
- **reflection** (Standard, depends ecs) — field-schema registry: `describe(name)` (registered schema, else inferred
  from a live value by `typeof`, else `[]`), `register(name, schema)`, `validate(name, partial)` (pure — the function
  `commands.setValidator` receives), `field.*` builders (number/boolean/string/color/select/vector2/readonly).
- **serialization** (Complex, depends ecs/storage/commands/reflection) — scene (de)serializer keyed by `EditorId`.
  `serialize()`/`deserialize()`, `save`/`load` (storage-backed JSON), `list`, `export`/`import`. Migrates via
  `storage`'s Migration chain, validates via `reflection`, routes loads through `commands.restore`. Emits `serialization:loaded`.
- **editor-selection** (Standard, depends ecs/renderer/camera/input) — viewport picking + selection. `enable`/`disable`
  flip Pixi `eventMode` on ONE camera pick layer (zero cost outside edit mode); `pickAt(screen)` resolves the topmost
  entity via a non-enumerable stamped handle; `select`/`toggle`/`clear`/`selected`/`isSelected`. Emits `editor-selection:changed`.
- **editor-history** (Standard, depends commands) — undo/redo. `applyTracked` wraps `commands.applyRaw` synchronously +
  records a bounded field-diff inverse; `undo`/`redo`; `beginGesture`/`endGesture` coalesce a drag into one entry; ring
  buffer capped at `maxDepth`; clears on `commands:restored` (via hooks). `canUndo`/`canRedo` polled on `changeEpoch`.
- **editor-gizmos** (Complex, depends renderer/camera/editor-selection/commands) — direct-manipulation translate gizmo
  (a Pixi overlay OUTSIDE the ECS). Drag deltas recomputed via `camera.screenToWorld` EVERY event (anti-drift); commits
  ≤2 `setField` on pointerup through `commands`, gesture-coalesced via an injected `setGestureSink`. MVP translate only.
  `enable`/`disable`/`setMode`/`setSnap`/`mode`/`setGestureSink`.
- **editor-runtime** (Complex, depends loop/scheduler/serialization/commands/tween/vfx/camera) — edit/play FSM via
  scheduler stage-gating over ONE world. `enterEdit` gates gameplay off, `enterPlay` snapshots + un-gates + starts the
  loop, `stop` restores the snapshot (`commands.restore`) THEN `reset()`s tween/vfx/camera THEN re-gates, `step` advances
  one frame. Emits `editor-runtime:modeChanged`. Does NOT gate at boot (pay-for-what-you-use).
- **editor-bridge** (Complex, depends all 9 editor seams + mcp; registered LAST) — the terminal facade, the one typed
  seam the Layer-3 web editor consumes. `snapshot(): EditorSnapshot` is immutable + poll-on-epoch (epoch mirrors
  `world.changeEpoch()`; entities memoized by epoch; selection/mode/canUndo/canRedo read fresh). Writes forward through
  the authority (`apply`/`setField` → editor-history.applyTracked → commands.applyRaw); `select`/`clearSelection`/`undo`/
  `redo`/`play`/`stop`/`step`/`save`/`load`/`describe` forward to the owning plugin. At onStart wires
  `commands.setValidator(reflection.validate)` and probes `mcp` readiness (the `editor://schema` + `editor:apply` mirror is a fast-follow).

Editor events (all coarse, user-gesture frequency — never per-frame): `commands:restored { source }`,
`serialization:loaded { name, entityCount }`, `editor-selection:changed { selected }`, `editor-runtime:modeChanged { mode }`.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 21. End-to-end example (scene → spawn → resources → loop → MCP)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```ts
import { createApp } from "game";
import { Container } from "pixi.js";
import { Time } from "game"; // or "../loop/resources" — the well-known Time token

const app = createApp({
  pluginConfigs: {
    renderer: { width: 1280, height: 720, background: 0x1099bb, mount: "#game" },
    loop:     { fixedDt: 1 / 60, maxStepsPerFrame: 5, autoStart: true },
    assets:   { basePath: "assets/", manifest: { hero: "sprites/hero.png" } },
    context:  { bindGameContext: true },
    mcp:      { transports: ["stdio", "http"], httpPort: 3333, enableMutations: true }
  }
});

await app.start();   // boots Pixi Application, attaches input listeners, binds resources, connects MCP

// Named components → discoverable by the mcp tools via componentByName.
const Velocity = app.ecs.defineComponent(() => ({ dx: 0, dy: 0 }), { name: "Velocity" });

// Capture the well-known resource tokens once.
const { assets, game } = app.context;

// A scene that pre-loads a bundle then spawns a transformed, rendered entity.
app.scene.define("level1", {
  bundle: { hero: "sprites/hero.png" },
  setup: async (world) => {
    const hero = world.spawn(                                  // tracked as owned by "level1"
      app.renderer.Transform({ x: 200, y: 200, rotation: 0, scaleX: 1, scaleY: 1 }),
      Velocity({ dx: 40, dy: 0 })
    );
    const sprite = await app.assets.sprite("hero");           // texture already cached by the bundle
    app.renderer.attach(hero, sprite);
  }
});

// Movement system (update stage); reaches services through `world`, not `app`.
app.scheduler.addSystem("update", (world, dt) => {
  const a = world.resource(assets);                           // AssetsApi — no closure over `app`
  const t = world.resource(app.loop.time);                   // { dt, elapsed, frame }
  void a; void t;
  world.query(app.renderer.Transform, Velocity).updateEach(([transform, v], entity) => {
    transform.x += v.dx * dt;
    app.renderer.markDirty(entity);                           // re-sync the view next "sync" tick
  });
});

await app.scene.load("level1");   // emits scene:loaded { name: "level1" }; loop is already driving frames

// Drive it deterministically (also what mcp loop:step does):
const { frame, elapsed, dt } = app.loop.step();              // → { frame, elapsed, dt }

// The MCP server is live: an agent can now (over app.mcp.httpEndpoint() or clientTransport()):
//   ecs:spawn { components: { Velocity: { dx: 80 } } }      // real components via componentByName
//   renderer:attach { id, spec: { shape: "circle", radius: 10, fill: 0xff0000 } }  // visible primitive
//   ecs:setComponent / ecs:removeComponent / input:key / loop:step / scene:load
//   renderer:tree / renderer:screenshot / scene:getInfo / ecs:query
//   game:reset  → emits the game:reset { reason: "mcp" } event
console.log(app.mcp.isRunning(), app.mcp.httpEndpoint(), app.mcp.toolNames());

await app.stop();   // destroys the Pixi Application (frees VRAM), removes listeners, closes MCP
```

**Listening for `game:reset`** (a consumer plugin re-initializing after an agent hard reset):

```ts
import { createPlugin, mcpPlugin } from "game";

const reinit = createPlugin("reinit", {
  depends: [mcpPlugin],
  hooks: (ctx) => ({
    "game:reset": ({ reason }) => {
      ctx.log.info(`game reset (${reason}) — reloading default scene`);
      // e.g. ctx.require(scenePlugin).load("level1")
    }
  })
});
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 22. Extension patterns
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- **Add a gameplay plugin:** `createPlugin("name", { depends: [ecsPlugin, schedulerPlugin], api, lifecycle })`.
  Resolve deps lazily with `ctx.require(...)` inside methods/lifecycle. Register systems via
  `ctx.require(schedulerPlugin).addSystem(stage, fn)` (or `ctx.require(ecsPlugin).addSystem`).
- **Share singletons with systems:** mint a `Resource<T>` with `world.defineResource(() => …)` and
  read it via `world.resource(token)` inside systems — no closure over `app`. Framework tokens
  (`Assets`, `GameContext`, `Time`) follow the same pattern.
- **Make components agent-addressable:** pass `opts.name` to `defineComponent` / `defineTag` so they
  appear in `componentNames()` / `componentsOf()` and are resolvable via `componentByName()` (and
  thus usable from the mcp `ecs:spawn` / `ecs:setComponent` / `ecs:removeComponent` tools).
- **Render without a Pixi handle:** use `renderer.attachPrimitive(entity, spec)` to add a visible
  rect/circle/line/polygon driven by the entity's `Transform` (this is what the mcp `renderer:attach`
  tool does).
- **React to milestones:** subscribe to `assets:loaded` / `scene:loaded` (framework) or `game:reset`
  (mcp) from a consumer plugin's `hooks` map.
- **Headless / agent-only host:** force `renderer: { headless: true }` (auto-detected under Node/Bun)
  to boot without a GPU; `loop.step()` still advances `Time` deterministically and returns its
  snapshot.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 23. Error handling & gotchas
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- `createApp(...)` is synchronous; `app.start()` / `app.stop()` are async and must be awaited.
- `app.renderer.Transform` throws if read **before** `app.start()` — the token is defined in
  `onStart`. Spawn renderable entities only after start (e.g. in a scene `setup`).
- `world.resource(token)` **throws** an actionable error when the resource is unset and has no
  factory; `world.getResource(token)` returns `undefined` instead. Framework resources (`Assets`,
  `GameContext`, `Time`) are unset until `app.start()` binds them. With
  `context: { bindGameContext: false }`, `Assets` is still bound but reading `GameContext` throws.
  Resource ops are immediate (never command-buffered) and not counted by `maxStructuralOpsWarn`.
- `world.componentByName(name)` returns `undefined` for unknown or anonymous names; on duplicate
  names the first-registered token wins. Prefer unique `opts.name` values.
- `app.scheduler.addSystem(stage, …)` with an unknown stage throws by default (`strictStages: true`);
  set `false` to downgrade to a logged warning + no-op.
- `app.assets.load(...)` rejects on failure by default; set `throwOnError: false` to log and resolve
  `undefined` instead (still typed `Texture` — do not rely on the value).
- `app.scene.load(name)` throws if `name` was never `define`d. (The mcp `scene:load` tool instead
  returns an `isError` listing `sceneNames()`.)
- `app.loop.step()` returns `{ frame, elapsed, dt }`; before `start` / after `stop` (no runtime) it
  returns `{ 0, 0, 0 }`. The return value is additive — void-context callers are unaffected.
- `app.input.keyDown/keyUp/keyPress` normalize aliases (`"Space"`/`"Spacebar"` → `" "`, `"Esc"` →
  `"Escape"`); query the snapshot with the canonical key. Injection is immediate, not buffered.
- `mcp` with `httpAuth: "bearer"` validates `bearerToken` as non-empty at startup; an empty token
  fails fast.
- `mcp` transports default by environment: `["inMemory"]` in a browser (in-page, no socket — reach
  the client via `app.mcp.clientTransport()`), `["stdio"]` under Node/Bun. The stdio transport is
  guarded on `process?.stdin`. `httpEndpoint()` is `undefined` unless the `"http"` transport is
  enabled. mcp mutating tools return `isError` on unknown component/scene names and dead entities.
- Headless renderer: `renderer.headless` (auto-detected `true` when there is no DOM, overridable)
  skips Pixi/GPU entirely — `state.app` stays `undefined`; `render()`/`getView()`/`getStage()` are
  no-ops; `screenshot()`/`tree()` resolve/return `undefined`; `attachPrimitive()` returns `false`;
  `Transform` is still defined and `sync` still runs. `input` and `loop` likewise degrade gracefully
  when DOM/rAF globals are absent.
- Structural mutations during iteration are deferred to the command buffer and applied at stage
  boundaries; outside iteration they apply immediately. Resource ops are always immediate.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 24. Scripts, requirements, layout
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Scripts (package.json, run with `bun`):

```
bun run build              # tsdown
bun run lint               # biome check . && eslint .
bun run lint:fix           # biome check --write . && eslint --fix .
bun run format             # biome format --write .
bun run test               # vitest run
bun run test:unit          # vitest run --project unit
bun run test:integration   # vitest run --project integration
bun run test:coverage      # vitest run --project unit --project integration --coverage
```

Requirements: Node `>= 24`, Bun `>= 1.3.14` (bun exclusively). TypeScript strict +
`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`. Dependencies: `@moku-labs/core`,
`@moku-labs/common`, `pixi.js@^8`; mcp adds `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `hono`, `zod`. Composable
with `@moku-labs/web`.

Layout:

```
src/config.ts              # Layer 1 — createCoreConfig, Config = {}, Events, createPlugin/createCore
src/index.ts               # Layer 2 — createCore(24 plugins: 16 runtime + 8 editor); exports createApp/createPlugin (+ createCore/createCoreConfig)
src/plugins/index.ts       # plugin barrel (instances + Ecs/Scheduler/Context/... type namespaces)
src/plugins/<name>/        # api.ts, types.ts, index.ts, lifecycle.ts / resources.ts / primitives.ts (where applicable), README.md, __tests__/
tests/                     # framework-level unit + integration tests
.planning/specs/           # design specs (intent)
```

License: MIT © moku-labs.
